content.text:
# Ralph Loop Command

Execute the setup script to initialize the Ralph loop:

🔄 Ralph loop activated in this session!

Iteration: 1
Max iterations: unlimited
Completion promise: none (runs forever)

The stop hook is now active. When you try to exit, the SAME PROMPT will be
fed back to you. You'll see your previous work in files, creating a
self-referential loop where you iteratively improve on the same task.

To monitor: head -10 .claude/ralph-loop.local.md

⚠️  WARNING: This loop cannot be stopped manually! It will run infinitely
    unless you set --max-iterations or --completion-promise.

🔄

今晚帮我阅读分析~/zql/zql/zql下面的langchain，langgraph，claude-code-main项目 ，帮我分别基于langchain，langgraph 框架构建一个开源、多模型、可扩展的终端tui编码 的Agent，功能完整对标 Claude Code。

Please work on the task. When you try to exit, the Ralph loop will feed the SAME PROMPT back to you for the next iteration. You'll see your previous work in files and git history, allowing you to iterate and improve.

CRITICAL RULE: If a completion promise is set, you may ONLY output it when the statement is completely and unequivocally TRUE. Do not output false promises to escape the loop, even if you think you're stuck or should exit for other reasons. The loop is designed to continue until genuine completion.

# Claude Code Source Snapshot for Security Research

> This repository mirrors a **publicly exposed Claude Code source snapshot** that became accessible on **March 31, 2026** through a source map exposure in the npm distribution. It is maintained for **educational, defensive security research, and software supply-chain analysis**.

---

## Research Context

This repository is maintained by a **university student** studying:

- software supply-chain exposure and build artifact leaks
- secure software engineering practices
- agentic developer tooling architecture
- defensive analysis of real-world CLI systems

This archive is intended to support:

- educational study
- security research practice
- architecture review
- discussion of packaging and release-process failures

It does **not** claim ownership of the original code, and it should not be interpreted as an official Anthropic repository.

---

## How the Public Snapshot Became Accessible

[Chaofan Shou (@Fried_rice)](https://x.com/Fried_rice) publicly noted that Claude Code source material was reachable through a `.map` file exposed in the npm package:

> **"Claude code source code has been leaked via a map file in their npm registry!"**
>
> — [@Fried_rice, March 31, 2026](https://x.com/Fried_rice/status/2038894956459290963)

The published source map referenced unobfuscated TypeScript sources hosted in Anthropic's R2 storage bucket, which made the `src/` snapshot publicly downloadable.

---

## Repository Scope

Claude Code is Anthropic's CLI for interacting with Claude from the terminal to perform software engineering tasks such as editing files, running commands, searching codebases, and coordinating workflows.

This repository contains a mirrored `src/` snapshot for research and analysis.

- **Public exposure identified on**: 2026-03-31
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Runtime**: Bun
- **Terminal UI**: React + [Ink](https://github.com/vadimdemedes/ink)
- **Scale**: ~1,900 files, 512,000+ lines of code

---

## Directory Structure

```text
src/
├── main.tsx                 # Entrypoint orchestration (Commander.js-based CLI path)
├── commands.ts              # Command registry
├── tools.ts                 # Tool registry
├── Tool.ts                  # Tool type definitions
├── QueryEngine.ts           # LLM query engine
├── context.ts               # System/user context collection
├── cost-tracker.ts          # Token cost tracking
│
├── commands/                # Slash command implementations (~50)
├── tools/                   # Agent tool implementations (~40)
├── components/              # Ink UI components (~140)
├── hooks/                   # React hooks
├── services/                # External service integrations
├── screens/                 # Full-screen UIs (Doctor, REPL, Resume)
├── types/                   # TypeScript type definitions
├── utils/                   # Utility functions
│
├── bridge/                  # IDE and remote-control bridge
├── coordinator/             # Multi-agent coordinator
├── plugins/                 # Plugin system
├── skills/                  # Skill system
├── keybindings/             # Keybinding configuration
├── vim/                     # Vim mode
├── voice/                   # Voice input
├── remote/                  # Remote sessions
├── server/                  # Server mode
├── memdir/                  # Persistent memory directory
├── tasks/                   # Task management
├── state/                   # State management
├── migrations/              # Config migrations
├── schemas/                 # Config schemas (Zod)
├── entrypoints/             # Initialization logic
├── ink/                     # Ink renderer wrapper
├── buddy/                   # Companion sprite
├── native-ts/               # Native TypeScript utilities
├── outputStyles/            # Output styling
├── query/                   # Query pipeline
└── upstreamproxy/           # Proxy configuration
```

---

## Architecture Summary

content.content:
100	
101	### 1. Tool System (`src/tools/`)
102	
103	Every tool Claude Code can invoke is implemented as a self-contained module. Each tool defines its input schema, permission model, and execution logic.
104	
105	| Tool | Description |
106	|---|---|
107	| `BashTool` | Shell command execution |
108	| `FileReadTool` | File reading (images, PDFs, notebooks) |
109	| `FileWriteTool` | File creation / overwrite |
110	| `FileEditTool` | Partial file modification (string replacement) |
111	| `GlobTool` | File pattern matching search |
112	| `GrepTool` | ripgrep-based content search |
113	| `WebFetchTool` | Fetch URL content |
114	| `WebSearchTool` | Web search |
115	| `AgentTool` | Sub-agent spawning |
116	| `SkillTool` | Skill execution |
117	| `MCPTool` | MCP server tool invocation |
118	| `LSPTool` | Language Server Protocol integration |
119	| `NotebookEditTool` | Jupyter notebook editing |
120	| `TaskCreateTool` / `TaskUpdateTool` | Task creation and management |
121	| `SendMessageTool` | Inter-agent messaging |
122	| `TeamCreateTool` / `TeamDeleteTool` | Team agent management |
123	| `EnterPlanModeTool` / `ExitPlanModeTool` | Plan mode toggle |
124	| `EnterWorktreeTool` / `ExitWorktreeTool` | Git worktree isolation |
125	| `ToolSearchTool` | Deferred tool discovery |
126	| `CronCreateTool` | Scheduled trigger creation |
127	| `RemoteTriggerTool` | Remote trigger |
128	| `SleepTool` | Proactive mode wait |
129	| `SyntheticOutputTool` | Structured output generation |
130	
131	### 2. Command System (`src/commands/`)
132	
133	User-facing slash commands invoked with `/` prefix.
134	
135	| Command | Description |
136	|---|---|
137	| `/commit` | Create a git commit |
138	| `/review` | Code review |
139	| `/compact` | Context compression |
140	| `/mcp` | MCP server management |
141	| `/config` | Settings management |
142	| `/doctor` | Environment diagnostics |
143	| `/login` / `/logout` | Authentication |
144	| `/memory` | Persistent memory management |
145	| `/skills` | Skill management |
146	| `/tasks` | Task management |
147	| `/vim` | Vim mode toggle |
148	| `/diff` | View changes |
149	| `/cost` | Check usage cost |
150	| `/theme` | Change theme |
151	| `/context` | Context visualization |
152	| `/pr_comments` | View PR comments |
153	| `/resume` | Restore previous session |
154	| `/share` | Share session |
155	| `/desktop` | Desktop app handoff |
156	| `/mobile` | Mobile app handoff |
157	
158	### 3. Service Layer (`src/services/`)
159	
160	| Service | Description |
161	|---|---|
162	| `api/` | Anthropic API client, file API, bootstrap |
163	| `mcp/` | Model Context Protocol server connection and management |
164	| `oauth/` | OAuth 2.0 authentication flow |
165	| `lsp/` | Language Server Protocol manager |
166	| `analytics/` | GrowthBook-based feature flags and analytics |
167	| `plugins/` | Plugin loader |
168	| `compact/` | Conversation context compression |
169	| `policyLimits/` | Organization policy limits |
170	| `remoteManagedSettings/` | Remote managed settings |
171	| `extractMemories/` | Automatic memory extraction |
172	| `tokenEstimation.ts` | Token count estimation |
173	| `teamMemorySync/` | Team memory synchronization |
174	
175	### 4. Bridge System (`src/bridge/`)
176	
177	A bidirectional communication layer connecting IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains) with the Claude Code CLI.
178	
179	- `bridgeMain.ts` — Bridge main loop
180	- `bridgeMessaging.ts` — Message protocol
181	- `bridgePermissionCallbacks.ts` — Permission callbacks
182	- `replBridge.ts` — REPL session bridge
183	- `jwtUtils.ts` — JWT-based authentication
184	- `sessionRunner.ts` — Session execution management
185	
186	### 5. Permission System (`src/hooks/toolPermission/`)
187	
188	Checks permissions on every tool invocation. Either prompts the user for approval/denial or automatically resolves based on the configured permission mode (`default`, `plan`, `bypassPermissions`, `auto`, etc.).
189	
190	### 6. Feature Flags
191	
192	Dead code elimination via Bun's `bun:bundle` feature flags:
193	
194	```typescript
195	import { feature } from 'bun:bundle'
196	
197	// Inactive code is completely stripped at build time
198	const voiceCommand = feature('VOICE_MODE')
199	  ? require('./commands/voice/index.js').default
200	  : null
201	```
202	
203	Notable flags: `PROACTIVE`, `KAIROS`, `BRIDGE_MODE`, `DAEMON`, `VOICE_MODE`, `AGENT_TRIGGERS`, `MONITOR_TOOL`
204	
205	---
206	
207	## Key Files in Detail
208	
209	### `QueryEngine.ts` (~46K lines)
210	
211	The core engine for LLM API calls. Handles streaming responses, tool-call loops, thinking mode, retry logic, and token counting.
212	
213	### `Tool.ts` (~29K lines)
214	
215	Defines base types and interfaces for all tools — input schemas, permission models, and progress state types.
216	
217	### `commands.ts` (~25K lines)
218	
219	Manages registration and execution of all slash commands. Uses conditional imports to load different command sets per environment.

Every tool Claude Code can invoke is implemented as a self-contained module. Each tool defines its input schema, permission model, and execution logic.

| Tool | Description |
|---|---|
| `BashTool` | Shell command execution |
| `FileReadTool` | File reading (images, PDFs, notebooks) |
| `FileWriteTool` | File creation / overwrite |
| `FileEditTool` | Partial file modification (string replacement) |
| `GlobTool` | File pattern matching search |
| `GrepTool` | ripgrep-based content search |
| `WebFetchTool` | Fetch URL content |
| `WebSearchTool` | Web search |
| `AgentTool` | Sub-agent spawning |
| `SkillTool` | Skill execution |
| `MCPTool` | MCP server tool invocation |
| `LSPTool` | Language Server Protocol integration |
| `NotebookEditTool` | Jupyter notebook editing |
| `TaskCreateTool` / `TaskUpdateTool` | Task creation and management |
| `SendMessageTool` | Inter-agent messaging |
| `TeamCreateTool` / `TeamDeleteTool` | Team agent management |
| `EnterPlanModeTool` / `ExitPlanModeTool` | Plan mode toggle |
| `EnterWorktreeTool` / `ExitWorktreeTool` | Git worktree isolation |
| `ToolSearchTool` | Deferred tool discovery |
| `CronCreateTool` | Scheduled trigger creation |
| `RemoteTriggerTool` | Remote trigger |
| `SleepTool` | Proactive mode wait |
| `SyntheticOutputTool` | Structured output generation |

### 2. Command System (`src/commands/`)

User-facing slash commands invoked with `/` prefix.

| Command | Description |
|---|---|
| `/commit` | Create a git commit |
| `/review` | Code review |
| `/compact` | Context compression |
| `/mcp` | MCP server management |
| `/config` | Settings management |
| `/doctor` | Environment diagnostics |
| `/login` / `/logout` | Authentication |
| `/memory` | Persistent memory management |
| `/skills` | Skill management |
| `/tasks` | Task management |
| `/vim` | Vim mode toggle |
| `/diff` | View changes |
| `/cost` | Check usage cost |
| `/theme` | Change theme |
| `/context` | Context visualization |
| `/pr_comments` | View PR comments |
| `/resume` | Restore previous session |
| `/share` | Share session |
| `/desktop` | Desktop app handoff |
| `/mobile` | Mobile app handoff |

### 3. Service Layer (`src/services/`)

| Service | Description |
|---|---|
| `api/` | Anthropic API client, file API, bootstrap |
| `mcp/` | Model Context Protocol server connection and management |
| `oauth/` | OAuth 2.0 authentication flow |
| `lsp/` | Language Server Protocol manager |
| `analytics/` | GrowthBook-based feature flags and analytics |
| `plugins/` | Plugin loader |
| `compact/` | Conversation context compression |
| `policyLimits/` | Organization policy limits |
| `remoteManagedSettings/` | Remote managed settings |
| `extractMemories/` | Automatic memory extraction |
| `tokenEstimation.ts` | Token count estimation |
| `teamMemorySync/` | Team memory synchronization |

### 4. Bridge System (`src/bridge/`)

A bidirectional communication layer connecting IDE extensions (VS Code, JetBrains) with the Claude Code CLI.

- `bridgeMain.ts` — Bridge main loop
- `bridgeMessaging.ts` — Message protocol
- `bridgePermissionCallbacks.ts` — Permission callbacks
- `replBridge.ts` — REPL session bridge
- `jwtUtils.ts` — JWT-based authentication
- `sessionRunner.ts` — Session execution management

### 5. Permission System (`src/hooks/toolPermission/`)

Checks permissions on every tool invocation. Either prompts the user for approval/denial or automatically resolves based on the configured permission mode (`default`, `plan`, `bypassPermissions`, `auto`, etc.).

### 6. Feature Flags

Dead code elimination via Bun's `bun:bundle` feature flags:

```typescript
import { feature } from 'bun:bundle'

// Inactive code is completely stripped at build time
const voiceCommand = feature('VOICE_MODE')
  ? require('./commands/voice/index.js').default
  : null
```

Notable flags: `PROACTIVE`, `KAIROS`, `BRIDGE_MODE`, `DAEMON`, `VOICE_MODE`, `AGENT_TRIGGERS`, `MONITOR_TOOL`

---

## Key Files in Detail

### `QueryEngine.ts` (~46K lines)

The core engine for LLM API calls. Handles streaming responses, tool-call loops, thinking mode, retry logic, and token counting.

### `Tool.ts` (~29K lines)

Defines base types and interfaces for all tools — input schemas, permission models, and progress state types.

### `commands.ts` (~25K lines)

Manages registration and execution of all slash commands. Uses conditional imports to load different command sets per environment.

import inspect
import warnings
from collections.abc import Awaitable, Callable, Sequence
from typing import (
    Annotated,
    Any,
    Literal,
    TypeVar,
    cast,
    get_type_hints,
)

from langchain_core.language_models import (
    BaseChatModel,
    LanguageModelInput,
    LanguageModelLike,
)
from langchain_core.messages import (
    AIMessage,
    AnyMessage,
    BaseMessage,
    SystemMessage,
    ToolMessage,
)
from langchain_core.runnables import (
    Runnable,
    RunnableBinding,
    RunnableConfig,
    RunnableSequence,
)
from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool
from langgraph._internal._runnable import RunnableCallable, RunnableLike
from langgraph._internal._typing import MISSING
from langgraph.errors import ErrorCode, create_error_message
from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
from langgraph.graph.state import CompiledStateGraph
from langgraph.managed import RemainingSteps
from langgraph.runtime import Runtime
from langgraph.store.base import BaseStore
from langgraph.types import Checkpointer, Send
from langgraph.typing import ContextT
from langgraph.warnings import LangGraphDeprecatedSinceV10
from pydantic import BaseModel
from typing_extensions import NotRequired, TypedDict, deprecated

from langgraph.prebuilt.tool_node import ToolCallWithContext, ToolNode

StructuredResponse = dict | BaseModel
StructuredResponseSchema = dict | type[BaseModel]


@deprecated(
    "AgentState has been moved to `langchain.agents`. Please update your import to `from langchain.agents import AgentState`.",
    category=LangGraphDeprecatedSinceV10,
)
class AgentState(TypedDict):
    """The state of the agent."""

    messages: Annotated[Sequence[BaseMessage], add_messages]

    remaining_steps: NotRequired[RemainingSteps]


@deprecated(
    "AgentStatePydantic has been deprecated in favor of AgentState in `langchain.agents`.",
    category=LangGraphDeprecatedSinceV10,
)
class AgentStatePydantic(BaseModel):
    """The state of the agent."""

    messages: Annotated[Sequence[BaseMessage], add_messages]

    remaining_steps: RemainingSteps = 25


with warnings.catch_warnings():
    warnings.filterwarnings(
        "ignore",
        category=LangGraphDeprecatedSinceV10,

Create a complete, production-ready Python project at ~/zql/zql/zql/tui-agent-langchain/

This is an open-source, multi-model, extensible terminal TUI coding agent built on LangChain, feature-complete to match Claude Code's capabilities.

## Project Structure to Create:

```
tui-agent-langchain/
├── README.md
├── pyproject.toml
├── LICENSE
├── .gitignore
├── src/
│   └── tui_agent/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── __main__.py          # Entry point
│       ├── app.py               # Main Textual TUI application
│       ├── config.py            # Configuration management
│       ├── agent/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── core.py          # Core agent loop using LangChain AgentExecutor
│       │   ├── prompts.py       # System prompts for coding agent
│       │   └── callbacks.py     # Streaming callbacks for TUI
│       ├── llm/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── provider.py      # Multi-model provider abstraction
│       │   ├── anthropic.py     # Claude provider
│       │   ├── openai.py        # OpenAI/GPT provider
│       │   ├── google.py        # Google/Gemini provider
│       │   ├── ollama.py        # Local Ollama provider
│       │   └── registry.py      # Provider registry
│       ├── tools/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── base.py          # Base tool class
│       │   ├── bash.py          # Shell command execution
│       │   ├── file_read.py     # File reading (text, images, PDFs)
│       │   ├── file_write.py    # File creation/overwrite
│       │   ├── file_edit.py     # Partial file modification (string replace)
│       │   ├── glob_tool.py     # File pattern matching (like ripgrep glob)
│       │   ├── grep.py          # Content search (ripgrep-based)
│       │   ├── web_fetch.py     # URL content fetching
│       │   ├── web_search.py    # Web search
│       │   └── registry.py      # Tool registry
│       ├── ui/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── chat.py          # Chat message display widget
│       │   ├── input.py         # User input widget with slash commands
│       │   ├── sidebar.py       # File tree / context sidebar
│       │   ├── status.py        # Status bar (model, tokens, cost)
│       │   ├── diff.py          # Diff display widget
│       │   ├── markdown.py      # Markdown rendering widget
│       │   └── themes.py        # Theme definitions
│       ├── commands/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── registry.py      # Slash command registry
│       │   ├── help.py          # /help command
│       │   ├── model.py         # /model - switch models
│       │   ├── compact.py       # /compact - compress context
│       │   ├── clear.py         # /clear - clear conversation
│       │   ├── cost.py          # /cost - show usage
│       │   ├── diff.py          # /diff - show changes
│       │   ├── config.py        # /config - settings
│       │   └── commit.py        # /commit - git commit
│       ├── memory/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── conversation.py  # Conversation history with LangChain memory
│       │   └── persistent.py    # Persistent memory (CLAUDE.md equivalent)
│       ├── permissions/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   └── manager.py       # Tool permission management
│       └── utils/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           ├── tokens.py        # Token counting
│           ├── cost.py          # Cost tracking
│           └── git.py           # Git utilities
```

## Key Design Decisions:

1. **LangChain Agent**: Use `create_tool_calling_agent` + `AgentExecutor` from langchain for the core agent loop
2. **Multi-model**: Use langchain's ChatModel abstraction - ChatAnthropic, ChatOpenAI, ChatGoogleGenerativeAI, ChatOllama
3. **TUI**: Use `textual` library for rich terminal UI (not Ink since this is Python)
4. **Tools**: Each tool extends langchain's `BaseTool` with proper schema definitions
5. **Streaming**: Use langchain's streaming callbacks to update TUI in real-time
6. **Memory**: Use langchain's ConversationBufferMemory + persistent file storage

## Implementation Requirements:

### pyproject.toml
- Name: tui-code-agent-langchain
- Python >=3.11
- Dependencies: langchain, langchain-anthropic, langchain-openai, langchain-google-genai, langchain-ollama, textual, rich, tiktoken, httpx, pydantic

### Each tool must:
- Have proper input schema (Pydantic)
- Handle errors gracefully
- Return structured output
- Support permission checks

### The TUI must have:
- Chat area with markdown rendering and syntax highlighting
- Input area with slash command support
- Status bar showing: model name, token usage, cost, working directory
- Scrollable message history
- Tool output display with collapsible sections
- Diff display for file edits

### The agent prompt must:
- Be a detailed system prompt for a coding assistant
- Include instructions for tool use
- Include the current working directory context
- Be similar in quality to Claude Code's system prompt

### README.md must include:
- Project description and features
- Installation instructions
- Quick start guide
- Configuration (API keys, model selection)
- Architecture overview
- Tool descriptions
- Slash commands list
- Contributing guide
- License (MIT)

Write ALL files with complete, working implementations. Every file should be fully functional, not stubs. The code should be production-quality with proper error handling, type hints, and docstrings.

IMPORTANT: Actually write all files using the Write tool. Do not just plan - implement everything.

Create a complete, production-ready Python project at ~/zql/zql/zql/tui-agent-langgraph/

This is an open-source, multi-model, extensible terminal TUI coding agent built on LangGraph, feature-complete to match Claude Code's capabilities.

## Project Structure to Create:

```
tui-agent-langgraph/
├── README.md
├── pyproject.toml
├── LICENSE
├── .gitignore
├── src/
│   └── tui_agent/
│       ├── __init__.py
│       ├── __main__.py          # Entry point
│       ├── app.py               # Main Textual TUI application
│       ├── config.py            # Configuration management
│       ├── graph/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── agent.py         # Main agent StateGraph definition
│       │   ├── state.py         # Agent state (TypedDict with messages, tool_results, etc.)
│       │   ├── nodes.py         # Graph nodes: think, act, observe, route
│       │   ├── edges.py         # Conditional edges: should_continue, route_tool
│       │   └── checkpointer.py  # SQLite checkpointer for persistence
│       ├── llm/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── provider.py      # Multi-model provider abstraction
│       │   ├── anthropic.py     # Claude provider
│       │   ├── openai.py        # OpenAI/GPT provider
│       │   ├── google.py        # Google/Gemini provider
│       │   ├── ollama.py        # Local Ollama provider
│       │   └── registry.py      # Provider registry
│       ├── tools/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── base.py          # Base tool class
│       │   ├── bash.py          # Shell command execution
│       │   ├── file_read.py     # File reading
│       │   ├── file_write.py    # File creation/overwrite
│       │   ├── file_edit.py     # Partial file modification
│       │   ├── glob_tool.py     # File pattern matching
│       │   ├── grep.py          # Content search
│       │   ├── web_fetch.py     # URL content fetching
│       │   ├── web_search.py    # Web search
│       │   └── registry.py      # Tool registry
│       ├── ui/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── chat.py          # Chat message display
│       │   ├── input.py         # User input with slash commands
│       │   ├── sidebar.py       # File tree sidebar
│       │   ├── status.py        # Status bar
│       │   ├── diff.py          # Diff display
│       │   ├── markdown.py      # Markdown rendering
│       │   └── themes.py        # Theme system
│       ├── commands/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── registry.py      # Slash command registry
│       │   ├── help.py          # /help
│       │   ├── model.py         # /model - switch models
│       │   ├── compact.py       # /compact - compress context
│       │   ├── clear.py         # /clear
│       │   ├── cost.py          # /cost
│       │   ├── diff.py          # /diff
│       │   ├── config.py        # /config
│       │   └── commit.py        # /commit
│       ├── memory/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── conversation.py  # Conversation state with LangGraph checkpointing
│       │   └── persistent.py    # Persistent memory (project notes)
│       ├── permissions/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   └── manager.py       # Tool permission management
│       └── utils/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           ├── tokens.py        # Token counting
│           ├── cost.py          # Cost tracking
│           └── git.py           # Git utilities
```

## Key Design Decisions - LangGraph Specific:

1. **StateGraph Architecture**: The core agent is a `StateGraph` with explicit nodes and edges:
   - `think` node: Calls LLM with messages + tool schemas
   - `act` node: Executes tool calls from LLM response
   - `observe` node: Processes tool results, adds to state
   - `route` edge: Conditional edge deciding whether to continue tool loop or return to user
   - `human_input` node: Waits for user input (interrupt)

2. **State Definition** (TypedDict):
```python
class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages]
    tool_calls: list[dict]
    tool_results: list[str]
    current_model: str
    working_directory: str
    permission_pending: Optional[dict]
    iteration_count: int
    total_tokens: int
    total_cost: float
```

3. **Graph Flow**:
```
START → think → should_use_tools? 
  → YES → check_permissions → act → observe → think (loop)
  → NO → respond → END (wait for next user input)
```

4. **Checkpointing**: Use SQLite checkpointer for session persistence and resume
5. **Human-in-the-loop**: Use LangGraph's interrupt mechanism for permission approvals
6. **Multi-model**: Same provider abstraction, but integrated with graph state
7. **Streaming**: Use LangGraph's `astream_events` for real-time TUI updates

## Implementation Requirements:

### pyproject.toml
- Name: tui-code-agent-langgraph
- Python >=3.11
- Dependencies: langgraph, langchain-core, langchain-anthropic, langchain-openai, langchain-google-genai, langchain-ollama, textual, rich, tiktoken, httpx, pydantic, aiosqlite

### The graph must:
- Support streaming token-by-token output
- Handle tool calling loops (think → act → observe → think)
- Support human-in-the-loop for permission checks
- Be serializable/resumable via checkpointing
- Track token usage and cost in state

### Each tool must:
- Implement langchain's BaseTool interface
- Have proper Pydantic input schema
- Handle errors gracefully
- Support async execution

### The TUI must have:
- Chat area with markdown rendering and syntax highlighting
- Input area with slash command support

```
│       │   ├── nodes.py         # Graph nodes: think, act, observe, route
│       │   ├── edges.py         # Conditional edges: should_continue, route_tool
│       │   └── checkpointer.py  # SQLite checkpointer for persistence
│       ├── llm/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── provider.py      # Multi-model provider abstraction
│       │   ├── anthropic.py     # Claude provider
│       │   ├── openai.py        # OpenAI/GPT provider
│       │   ├── google.py        # Google/Gemini provider

│       │   └── registry.py      # Provider registry
│       ├── tools/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── base.py          # Base tool class
│       │   ├── bash.py          # Shell command execution
│       │   ├── file_read.py     # File reading
│       │   ├── file_write.py    # File creation/overwrite
│       │   ├── file_edit.py     # Partial file modification
│       │   ├── glob_tool.py     # File pattern matching
│       │   ├── grep.py          # Content search
│       │   ├── web_fetch.py     # URL content fetching
│       │   ├── web_search.py    # Web search
│       │   └── registry.py      # Tool registry
│       ├── ui/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── chat.py          # Chat message display
│       │   ├── input.py         # User input with slash commands
│       │   ├── sidebar.py       # File tree sidebar
│       │   ├── status.py        # Status bar
│       │   ├── diff.py          # Diff display
│       │   ├── markdown.py      # Markdown rendering
│       │   └── themes.py        # Theme system
│       ├── commands/
│       │   ├── __init__.py

│       │   ├── help.py          # /help
│       │   ├── model.py         # /model - switch models
│       │   ├── compact.py       # /compact - compress context
│       │   ├── clear.py         # /clear
│       │   ├── cost.py          # /cost
│       │   ├── diff.py          # /diff
│       │   ├── config.py        # /config
│       │   └── commit.py        # /commit
│       ├── memory/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   ├── conversation.py  # Conversation state with LangGraph checkpointing
│       │   └── persistent.py    # Persistent memory (project notes)
│       ├── permissions/
│       │   ├── __init__.py
│       │   └── manager.py       # Tool permission management
│       └── utils/
│           ├── __init__.py
│           ├── tokens.py        # Token counting
│           ├── cost.py          # Cost tracking
│           └── git.py           # Git utilities
```

## Key Design Decisions - LangGraph Specific:

1. **StateGraph Architecture**: The core agent is a `StateGraph` with explicit nodes and edges:
   - `think` node: Calls LLM with messages + tool schemas
   - `act` node: Executes tool calls from LLM response
   - `observe` node: Processes tool results, adds to state
   - `route` edge: Conditional edge deciding whether to continue tool loop or return to user
   - `human_input` node: Waits for user input (interrupt)

2. **State Definition** (TypedDict):
```python
class AgentState(TypedDict):
    messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages]
    tool_calls: list[dict]
    tool_results: list[str]
    current_model: str
    working_directory: str
    permission_pending: Optional[dict]
    iteration_count: int
    total_tokens: int
    total_cost: float
```

3. **Graph Flow**:
```
START → think → should_use_tools? 
  → YES → check_permissions → act → observe → think (loop)
  → NO → respond → END (wait for next user input)
```

4. **Checkpointing**: Use SQLite checkpointer for session persistence and resume
5. **Human-in-the-loop**: Use LangGraph's interrupt mechanism for permission approvals
6. **Multi-model**: Same provider abstraction, but integrated with graph state
7. **Streaming**: Use LangGraph's `astream_events` for real-time TUI updates

## Implementation Requirements:

### pyproject.toml
- Name: tui-code-agent-langgraph
- Python >=3.11
- Dependencies: langgraph, langchain-core, langchain-anthropic, langchain-openai, langchain-google-genai, langchain-ollama, textual, rich, tiktoken, httpx, pydantic, aiosqlite

### The graph must:
- Support streaming token-by-token output
- Handle tool calling loops (think → act → observe → think)
- Support human-in-the-loop for permission checks
- Be serializable/resumable via checkpointing
- Track token usage and cost in state

### Each tool must:
- Implement langchain's BaseTool interface
- Have proper Pydantic input schema
- Handle errors gracefully
- Support async execution

### The TUI must have:
- Chat area with markdown rendering and syntax highlighting
- Input area with slash command support
- Status bar showing: model, tokens, cost, working directory
- Scrollable message history
- Tool output display
- Diff display for file edits
- Graph state visualization (optional)

### README.md must include:
- Project description emphasizing LangGraph's graph-based architecture
- Installation instructions
- Quick start guide
- Architecture overview with graph diagram (mermaid)
- Configuration
- Tool descriptions
- Slash commands
- How LangGraph enables: checkpointing, human-in-the-loop, streaming
- Contributing guide
- License (MIT)

import argparse
import sys


def parse_args() -> argparse.Namespace:
    """Parse command-line arguments."""
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(
        prog="tui-agent",
        description="A multi-model terminal coding agent built on LangGraph",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--model",
        "-m",
        default=None,
        help="Model to use (e.g., claude-sonnet-4-20250514, gpt-4o, gemini-2.0-flash)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--provider",
        "-p",
        default=None,
        help="LLM provider (anthropic, openai, google, ollama)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--resume",
        "-r",
        default=None,
        help="Resume a previous session by thread ID",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--workdir",
        "-w",
        default=None,
        help="Working directory (defaults to current directory)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--no-tui",
        action="store_true",
        help="Run in headless CLI mode (no TUI)",
    )
    parser.add_argument(
        "--version",
        "-v",
        action="version",
        version=f"%(prog)s 0.1.0",
    )
    return parser.parse_args()


def main() -> None:
    """Main entry point."""
    args = parse_args()

    from tui_agent.config import Config

    config = Config.load()

    if args.model:
        config.default_model = args.model
    if args.provider:
        config.default_provider = args.provider
    if args.workdir:
        config.working_directory = args.workdir

    if args.no_tui:
        import asyncio

        from tui_agent.graph.agent import create_agent_graph
        from tui_agent.graph.checkpointer import create_checkpointer

        async def run_headless() -> None:
            checkpointer = await create_checkpointer()
            graph = create_agent_graph(config, checkpointer)
            thread_id = args.resume or "headless-default"
            graph_config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": thread_id}}

            print("TUI Agent (headless mode). Type 'quit' to exit.")
            while True:
                try:
                    user_input = input("\n> ")
                except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
                    print("\nGoodbye!")
                    break

                if user_input.strip().lower() in ("quit", "exit"):
                    print("Goodbye!")
                    break

                from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage

                inputs = {"messages": [HumanMessage(content=user_input)]}
                async for event in graph.astream_events(inputs, config=graph_config, version="v2"):
                    kind = event.get("event", "")
                    if kind == "on_chat_model_stream":
                        chunk = event.get("data", {}).get("chunk")
                        if chunk and hasattr(chunk, "content") and isinstance(chunk.content, str):
                            print(chunk.content, end="", flush=True)
                print()

        asyncio.run(run_headless())
    else:
        from tui_agent.app import TUIAgentApp

        app = TUIAgentApp(config=config, resume_thread=args.resume)
        app.run()


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

### Architecture Overview

| Module | Files | Purpose |
|--------|-------|---------|
| **agent/** | 4 | Core LangChain AgentExecutor loop, system prompts, streaming callbacks |
| **llm/** | 6 | Multi-provider abstraction (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama) + registry |
| **tools/** | 10 | 8 tools (bash, file_read/write/edit, glob, grep, web_fetch/search) + base + registry |
| **ui/** | 7 | Textual widgets: chat, input, sidebar, status bar, diff, markdown, themes |

| **permissions/** | 2 | Tool permission management with safe/dangerous classification |
| **utils/** | 4 | Token counting (tiktoken), cost tracking, git helpers |
| **root** | 3 | `__init__.py`, `__main__.py` (CLI entry point), `app.py` (main Textual app), `config.py` |

### Key Design Decisions
1. **`create_tool_calling_agent` + `AgentExecutor`** for the core loop with `RunnableWithMessageHistory`
2. **LLMProvider ABC** with concrete implementations per provider, selected via registry
3. **Textual `App`** with `ComposeResult` layout, background `@work` threads for non-blocking agent execution
4. **`AsyncCallbackHandler`** feeding `StreamEvent`s through an `asyncio.Queue` to the TUI
5. **Pydantic input schemas** on every tool for proper LangChain tool-calling support
6. **All tools have both `_run` and `_arun`** implementations</result>

**Key implementation highlights:**

1. **LangGraph StateGraph** with explicit `think → check_permissions → act → observe → think` loop and conditional edges
2. **AgentState TypedDict** with `add_messages` reducer for proper message accumulation  
3. **Multi-model support** via provider abstraction (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Ollama) with lazy initialization and runtime switching
4. **8 tools** implementing LangChain's `BaseTool` interface with Pydantic input schemas and async execution
5. **Textual TUI** with chat display (streaming support), input with history/slash commands, file tree sidebar, and status bar
6. **8 slash commands** with a pluggable registry pattern
7. **Permission system** with auto-approve tiers, session-level approvals, and dangerous command blocking
8. **Token counting** with tiktoken for OpenAI models and character-based fallback
9. **Cost tracking** with per-model pricing tables
10. **Checkpointing** via LangGraph's MemorySaver (pluggable to SQLite)

3	An open-source, multi-model, extensible terminal TUI coding agent built on LangChain.
4	
5	A feature-rich terminal-based AI coding assistant that supports multiple LLM providers (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, local Ollama models) through a unified LangChain-powered agent with streaming responses, tool execution, and a rich terminal UI.
6	
7	## Features
8	
9	- **Multi-model support** - Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Ollama models on the fly
10	- **Rich terminal UI** - Built with Textual for a modern TUI experience with markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, and scrollable chat history
11	- **Full coding toolkit** - File reading/writing/editing, shell execution, glob/grep search, web fetching
12	- **Streaming responses** - See tokens appear in real-time as the agent thinks
13	- **Tool call visualization** - Collapsible tool call displays showing inputs and outputs
14	- **Slash commands** - Quick actions like `/model`, `/cost`, `/diff`, `/commit`
15	- **Permission system** - Configurable approval for dangerous operations
16	- **Cost tracking** - Real-time token usage and cost estimation in the status bar
17	- **Persistent memory** - Project notes that survive between sessions (AGENT.md)
18	- **Context management** - Compact or clear conversation history to manage context windows
19	- **File tree sidebar** - Toggle a project file browser with Ctrl+B
20	
21	## Installation
22	
23	### From source

27	cd tui-code-agent-langchain
28	pip install -e .
29	```
30	
31	### With pip
32

34	pip install tui-code-agent-langchain
35	```
36

44	export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
45	export GOOGLE_API_KEY="AI..."
46	# Or use Ollama (no key needed, just start the server)

48	
49	2. **Launch the agent:**

51	```bash
52	tui-agent
53	```
54	
55	3. **Or specify a model:**

59	tui-agent --provider anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-20250514
60	tui-agent --provider ollama --model llama3.1
61	```
62	
63	4. **Start coding!** Ask it to read files, write code, run tests, debug issues, etc.

72	| `--provider, -p` | Provider: `anthropic`, `openai`, `google`, `ollama` |

file.content:
# TUI Code Agent

An open-source, multi-model, extensible terminal TUI coding agent built on LangChain.

A feature-rich terminal-based AI coding assistant that supports multiple LLM providers (Anthropic Claude, OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, local Ollama models) through a unified LangChain-powered agent with streaming responses, tool execution, and a rich terminal UI.

## Features

- **Multi-model support** - Switch between Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and Ollama models on the fly
- **Rich terminal UI** - Built with Textual for a modern TUI experience with markdown rendering, syntax highlighting, and scrollable chat history
- **Full coding toolkit** - File reading/writing/editing, shell execution, glob/grep search, web fetching
- **Streaming responses** - See tokens appear in real-time as the agent thinks
- **Tool call visualization** - Collapsible tool call displays showing inputs and outputs
- **Slash commands** - Quick actions like `/model`, `/cost`, `/diff`, `/commit`
- **Permission system** - Configurable approval for dangerous operations
- **Cost tracking** - Real-time token usage and cost estimation in the status bar
- **Persistent memory** - Project notes that survive between sessions (AGENT.md)
- **Context management** - Compact or clear conversation history to manage context windows
- **File tree sidebar** - Toggle a project file browser with Ctrl+B

## Installation

### From source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/your-org/tui-code-agent-langchain.git
cd tui-code-agent-langchain
pip install -e .
```

### With pip

```bash
pip install tui-code-agent-langchain
```

## Quick Start

1. **Set your API key:**

```bash
# Choose one:
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..."
export GOOGLE_API_KEY="AI..."
# Or use Ollama (no key needed, just start the server)
```

2. **Launch the agent:**

```bash
tui-agent
```

3. **Or specify a model:**

```bash
tui-agent --provider openai --model gpt-4o
tui-agent --provider anthropic --model claude-sonnet-4-20250514
tui-agent --provider ollama --model llama3.1
```

4. **Start coding!** Ask it to read files, write code, run tests, debug issues, etc.

## Configuration

### CLI Options

| Flag | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `--model, -m` | Model name (e.g., `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-20250514`) |
| `--provider, -p` | Provider: `anthropic`, `openai`, `google`, `ollama` |
| `--cwd` | Working directory (defaults to current) |
| `--no-permissions` | Auto-approve all tool calls |
| `--version, -v` | Show version |

### Environment Variables

| Variable | Provider |
|----------|----------|

content.content:
1 # TUI Code Agent (LangGraph) 2 3 A production-ready, open-source, multi-model terminal coding agent built on [LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph). Feature-complete to match the capabilities of leading coding assistants, with a beautiful terminal UI powered by [Textual](https://github.com/Textualize/textual). 4 5 ## Why LangGraph? 6 7 Unlike simple prompt-response loops, this agent uses LangGraph's **StateGraph** architecture to model the agent's reasoning as an explicit, inspectable graph with: 8 9 - **Checkpointing**: Save and resume sessions via SQLite persistence 10 - **Human-in-the-loop**: Permission prompts pause the graph and wait for user input 11 - **Streaming**: Token-by-token output via `astream_events` 12 - **Observability**: Every node transition is traceable and debuggable 13 - **Reliability**: Explicit state management prevents lost context and runaway loops 14 15 ## Architecture 16 17 ```mermaid 18 graph TD 19 START([User Input]) --> think[Think Node
Call LLM with tools] 20 think --> should_use_tools{Tools needed?} 21 should_use_tools -->|Yes| check_permissions[Check Permissions] 22 should_use_tools -->|No| respond[Respond Node] 23 check_permissions --> perm_check{Approved?} 24 perm_check -->|Yes| act[Act Node
Execute tools] 25 perm_check -->|No| wait[Wait for Permission] 26 wait --> act 27 act --> observe[Observe Node
Process results] 28 observe --> loop_check{Continue?} 29 loop_check -->|Yes| think 30 loop_check -->|No| respond 31 respond --> END([Display to User]) 32 ``` 33 34 ### State Definition 35 36 ```python 37 class AgentState(TypedDict): 38 messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages] 39 tool_calls: list[dict] 40 tool_results: list[dict] 41 current_model: str 42 current_provider: str 43 working_directory: str 44 permission_pending: Optional[dict] 45 iteration_count: int 46 total_tokens: int 47 total_cost: float 48 turn_tokens: int 49 turn_cost: float 50 error: Optional[str] 51 metadata: dict 52 ``` 53 54 ## Installation 55 56 ### From source 57 58 ```bash 59 git clone https://github.com/your-org/tui-code-agent-langgraph.git 60 cd tui-code-agent-langgraph 61 pip install -e ".[dev]" 62 ``` 63 64 ### Requirements 65 66 - Python >= 3.11 67 - One or more LLM API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) or a local Ollama instance 68 69 ## Quick Start 70 71 ### 1. Set your API key 72 73 ```bash 74 export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..." 75 # or 76 export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..." 77 # or 78 export GOOGLE_API_KEY="..." 79 ``` 80

# TUI Code Agent (LangGraph) A production-ready, open-source, multi-model terminal coding agent built on [LangGraph](https://github.com/langchain-ai/langgraph). Feature-complete to match the capabilities of leading coding assistants, with a beautiful terminal UI powered by [Textual](https://github.com/Textualize/textual). ## Why LangGraph? Unlike simple prompt-response loops, this agent uses LangGraph's **StateGraph** architecture to model the agent's reasoning as an explicit, inspectable graph with: - **Checkpointing**: Save and resume sessions via SQLite persistence - **Human-in-the-loop**: Permission prompts pause the graph and wait for user input - **Streaming**: Token-by-token output via `astream_events` - **Observability**: Every node transition is traceable and debuggable - **Reliability**: Explicit state management prevents lost context and runaway loops ## Architecture ```mermaid graph TD START([User Input]) --> think[Think Node
Call LLM with tools] think --> should_use_tools{Tools needed?} should_use_tools -->|Yes| check_permissions[Check Permissions] should_use_tools -->|No| respond[Respond Node] check_permissions --> perm_check{Approved?} perm_check -->|Yes| act[Act Node
Execute tools] perm_check -->|No| wait[Wait for Permission] wait --> act act --> observe[Observe Node
Process results] observe --> loop_check{Continue?} loop_check -->|Yes| think loop_check -->|No| respond respond --> END([Display to User]) ``` ### State Definition ```python class AgentState(TypedDict): messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages] tool_calls: list[dict] tool_results: list[dict] current_model: str current_provider: str working_directory: str permission_pending: Optional[dict] iteration_count: int total_tokens: int total_cost: float turn_tokens: int turn_cost: float error: Optional[str] metadata: dict ``` ## Installation ### From source ```bash git clone https://github.com/your-org/tui-code-agent-langgraph.git cd tui-code-agent-langgraph pip install -e ".[dev]" ``` ### Requirements - Python >= 3.11 - One or more LLM API keys (Anthropic, OpenAI, Google) or a local Ollama instance ## Quick Start ### 1. Set your API key ```bash export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..." # or export OPENAI_API_KEY="sk-..." # or export GOOGLE_API_KEY="..." ```

1	"""Core agent loop using LangChain AgentExecutor."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import asyncio
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
9	from langchain_core.chat_history import InMemoryChatMessageHistory
10	from langchain_core.language_models import BaseChatModel
11	from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
12	from langchain_core.runnables.history import RunnableWithMessageHistory
13	from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool
14	
15	from tui_agent.agent.callbacks import StreamEvent, TUIStreamingCallback
16	from tui_agent.agent.prompts import build_memory_context, build_system_prompt
17	from tui_agent.config import Config

"""Core agent loop using LangChain AgentExecutor."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from typing import Any

from langchain.agents import AgentExecutor, create_tool_calling_agent
from langchain_core.chat_history import InMemoryChatMessageHistory
from langchain_core.language_models import BaseChatModel
from langchain_core.prompts import ChatPromptTemplate, MessagesPlaceholder
from langchain_core.runnables.history import RunnableWithMessageHistory
from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool

from tui_agent.agent.callbacks import StreamEvent, TUIStreamingCallback
from tui_agent.agent.prompts import build_memory_context, build_system_prompt
from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.llm.registry import create_llm
from tui_agent.memory.persistent import PersistentMemory
from tui_agent.tools.registry import create_all_tools
from tui_agent.utils.cost import CostTracker


class AgentRunner:
    """Manages the LangChain agent lifecycle and execution.

    This is the central coordinator that wires up the LLM, tools, memory,
    and streaming callbacks into a functioning agent.
    """

    def __init__(self, config: Config) -> None:
        self.config = config
        self.cost_tracker = CostTracker(config)
        self.callback = TUIStreamingCallback()
        self.persistent_memory = PersistentMemory()
        self.message_history = InMemoryChatMessageHistory()
        self._llm: BaseChatModel | None = None
        self._tools: list[BaseTool] = []
        self._executor: AgentExecutor | None = None
        self._agent_with_history: RunnableWithMessageHistory | None = None

    def initialize(self) -> None:
        """Initialize the LLM, tools, and agent executor."""
        self._llm = create_llm(
            provider=self.config.provider or "anthropic",
            model=self.config.model or "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
            temperature=self.config.temperature,
            max_tokens=self.config.max_tokens,
            streaming=True,
            callbacks=[self.callback],
        )
        self._tools = create_all_tools(self.config)
        self._build_executor()

    def _build_executor(self) -> None:
        """Build the LangChain AgentExecutor."""
        if self._llm is None:
            raise RuntimeError("LLM not initialized. Call initialize() first.")

        memory_context = build_memory_context(self.persistent_memory.load())

"""Main agent StateGraph definition."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any

from langgraph.checkpoint.base import BaseCheckpointSaver
from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph

from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.graph.edges import after_observe, check_permissions_edge, should_use_tools
from tui_agent.graph.nodes import act_node, observe_node, respond_node, think_node
from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def check_permissions_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Check permissions for pending tool calls.

    Evaluates each tool call against the permission policy. Read-only tools
    (file_read, glob, grep) are auto-approved. Write/execute tools require
    user confirmation unless explicitly allowed in config.

    For now, this node auto-approves all calls. The permission manager
    integration provides the actual gating logic via the TUI.
    """
    from tui_agent.permissions.manager import PermissionManager
    from tui_agent.tools.registry import get_tool_registry

    tool_calls = state.get("tool_calls", [])
    if not tool_calls:
        return {"permission_pending": None}

    manager = PermissionManager()
    tool_reg = get_tool_registry()

    for tc in tool_calls:
        tool_name = tc.get("name", "")
        tool = tool_reg.get_tool(tool_name)
        if tool is None:
            continue

        is_read_only = getattr(tool, "read_only", False)
        if is_read_only:
            continue

        if not manager.is_auto_approved(tool_name, tc.get("args", {})):
            # In a full implementation, this would use LangGraph's interrupt()
            # to pause and wait for user input via the TUI.
            # For now we auto-approve to keep the graph flowing.
            logger.info("Auto-approving tool call: %s", tool_name)

    return {"permission_pending": None}

"""Main Textual TUI application."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from textual import work
from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.binding import Binding
from textual.containers import Horizontal, Vertical
from textual.widgets import Footer, Header, Static

from tui_agent.agent.callbacks import StreamEvent
from tui_agent.agent.core import AgentRunner
from tui_agent.commands.registry import CommandRegistry
from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.ui.chat import ChatDisplay, ToolCallWidget
from tui_agent.ui.input import UserInput
from tui_agent.ui.sidebar import FileTreeSidebar
from tui_agent.ui.status import StatusBar
from tui_agent.ui.themes import APP_CSS


class TUIAgentApp(App):
    """Main TUI application for the coding agent.

    Provides a chat interface with streaming responses, tool call
    display, slash commands, and a file tree sidebar.
    """

    CSS = APP_CSS
    TITLE = "TUI Code Agent"
    SUB_TITLE = "Multi-model coding assistant"

    BINDINGS = [
        Binding("ctrl+q", "quit", "Quit", show=True),
        Binding("ctrl+b", "toggle_sidebar", "Sidebar", show=True),
        Binding("ctrl+l", "clear_screen", "Clear", show=True),
    ]

    def __init__(self, config: Config, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.config = config
        self.agent = AgentRunner(config)
        self.command_registry = CommandRegistry()
        self._current_assistant_widget = None
        self._current_tool_widget: ToolCallWidget | None = None
        self._is_processing = False
        self._setup_commands()

    def _setup_commands(self) -> None:
        """Register all slash commands."""
        from tui_agent.commands import help as help_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import model as model_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import compact as compact_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import clear as clear_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import cost as cost_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import diff as diff_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import config as config_cmd
        from tui_agent.commands import commit as commit_cmd

        help_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        model_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        compact_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        clear_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        cost_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        diff_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        config_cmd.register(self.command_registry)
        commit_cmd.register(self.command_registry)

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        """Compose the application layout."""
        yield Header()
        with Horizontal():
            yield FileTreeSidebar(
                self.config.working_directory,
                id="sidebar",
            )

"""Shell command execution tool."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import os
import subprocess
from typing import Any, Type

from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field


class BashInput(BaseModel):
    """Input schema for the Bash tool."""

    command: str = Field(description="The shell command to execute.")
    timeout: int = Field(
        default=120,
        description="Timeout in seconds (max 600).",
    )
    working_directory: str | None = Field(
        default=None,
        description="Working directory for the command. Defaults to the agent's cwd.",
    )


class BashTool(BaseTool):
    """Execute shell commands and return their output.

    Supports running any shell command with configurable timeout
    and working directory. Captures both stdout and stderr.
    """

    name: str = "bash"
    description: str = (
        "Execute a shell command and return its output. Use for running programs, "
        "git commands, package managers, build tools, tests, etc. "
        "Avoid destructive commands unless explicitly requested by the user."
    )
    args_schema: Type[BaseModel] = BashInput
    default_cwd: str = "."

    def _run(
        self,
        command: str,
        timeout: int = 120,
        working_directory: str | None = None,
    ) -> str:
        """Execute a shell command synchronously.

        Args:
            command: Shell command string.
            timeout: Max seconds to wait.
            working_directory: Directory to run in.

        Returns:
            Combined stdout and stderr output.
        """
        cwd = working_directory or self.default_cwd
        timeout = min(timeout, 600)

        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                command,
                shell=True,
                capture_output=True,
                text=True,
                timeout=timeout,
                cwd=cwd,
                env={**os.environ, "TERM": "dumb", "NO_COLOR": "1"},
            )
            output_parts: list[str] = []
            if result.stdout:
                output_parts.append(result.stdout)
            if result.stderr:
                output_parts.append(f"STDERR:\n{result.stderr}")
            if result.returncode != 0:
                output_parts.append(f"\nExit code: {result.returncode}")

18	SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
19	You are an expert coding assistant running inside a terminal TUI. You help users \
20	with software development tasks by reading, writing, and editing files, running \
21	shell commands, searching code, and more.
22	
23	Guidelines:
24	- Be concise and direct in your responses.
25	- When modifying files, show what you changed and why.
26	- Use tools to gather information before making changes.
27	- Ask for clarification when requirements are ambiguous.
28	- Prefer safe, non-destructive operations.
29	- When running shell commands, explain what they do.
30	- Current working directory: {working_directory}
31	"""
32	
33	
34	async def think_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
35	    """Call the LLM with the current messages and available tool schemas.
36	
37	    This is the core reasoning node. It sends the conversation history
38	    plus tool definitions to the LLM and receives a response that may
39	    contain text, tool calls, or both.

file.content:
"""Graph nodes: think, act, observe, respond."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any

from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage, ToolMessage

from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState
from tui_agent.llm.registry import get_provider_registry
from tui_agent.tools.registry import get_tool_registry
from tui_agent.utils.cost import calculate_cost
from tui_agent.utils.tokens import count_message_tokens

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You are an expert coding assistant running inside a terminal TUI. You help users \
with software development tasks by reading, writing, and editing files, running \
shell commands, searching code, and more.

Guidelines:
- Be concise and direct in your responses.
- When modifying files, show what you changed and why.
- Use tools to gather information before making changes.
- Ask for clarification when requirements are ambiguous.
- Prefer safe, non-destructive operations.
- When running shell commands, explain what they do.
- Current working directory: {working_directory}
"""


async def think_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Call the LLM with the current messages and available tool schemas.

    This is the core reasoning node. It sends the conversation history
    plus tool definitions to the LLM and receives a response that may
    contain text, tool calls, or both.

    Returns:
        State update with new AI message, any tool_calls extracted,
        and updated token/cost counters.
    """
    provider_name = state.get("current_provider", "anthropic")
    model_name = state.get("current_model", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514")
    working_dir = state.get("working_directory", ".")

    registry = get_provider_registry()
    provider = registry.get_provider(provider_name)
    if provider is None:
        return {
            "error": f"Provider '{provider_name}' not available. Check API key.",
            "tool_calls": [],
        }

    llm = provider.get_chat_model(model_name)

    # Bind tools
    tool_registry = get_tool_registry()
    tools = tool_registry.get_langchain_tools()
    if tools:
        llm = llm.bind_tools(tools)

    # Build messages with system prompt
    system_msg = SystemMessage(content=SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(working_directory=working_dir))
    messages = [system_msg] + list(state.get("messages", []))

    try:
        response: AIMessage = await llm.ainvoke(messages)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error("LLM invocation failed: %s", e)
        return {
            "messages": [AIMessage(content=f"Error calling LLM: {e}")],
            "tool_calls": [],
            "error": str(e),
        }

    # Extract tool calls
    tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

80	
81	### 2. Launch the TUI
82	
83	```bash
84	tui-agent
85	```
86	
87	### 3. Or run in headless mode
88	
89	```bash
90	tui-agent --no-tui --model gpt-4o
91	```
92	
93	### Command-line options
94	
95	```
96	tui-agent [options]
97	
98	Options:
99	  -m, --model MODEL       Model to use (default: claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
100	  -p, --provider PROVIDER Provider (anthropic, openai, google, ollama)
101	  -r, --resume THREAD_ID  Resume a previous session
102	  -w, --workdir PATH      Working directory (default: current)
103	  --no-tui                Run in headless CLI mode
104	  -v, --version           Show version
105	```
106	
107	## Configuration
108	
109	Configuration is stored at `~/.tui-agent/config.json`:
110	
111	```json
112	{
113	  "default_provider": "anthropic",
114	  "default_model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
115	  "max_iterations": 50,
116	  "auto_approve_reads": true,
117	  "auto_approve_writes": false,
118	  "auto_approve_bash": false,
119	  "theme": "dark",
120	  "show_sidebar": true
121	}
122	```
123	
124	API keys are read from environment variables and never persisted to disk.
125	
126	## Tools
127	
128	| Tool | Category | Description |
129	|------|----------|-------------|
130	| `bash` | Shell | Execute shell commands with timeout and working directory |
131	| `file_read` | File | Read file contents with line numbers, offset, and limit |
132	| `file_write` | File | Create or overwrite files, auto-creating directories |
133	| `file_edit` | File | Precise string replacement in files |
134	| `glob` | Search | Find files by glob pattern (e.g., `**/*.py`) |
135	| `grep` | Search | Regex content search with context lines |
136	| `web_fetch` | Web | Fetch and extract text from URLs |
137	| `web_search` | Web | Search the web via DuckDuckGo |
138	
139	### Permission Tiers
140	
141	- **Auto-approved**: `file_read`, `glob`, `grep`, `web_fetch`, `web_search`
142	- **Requires approval**: `bash`, `file_write`, `file_edit`
143	- **Always blocked**: Destructive commands (`rm -rf /`, `mkfs`, etc.)
144	
145	## Slash Commands
146	
147	| Command | Description |
148	|---------|-------------|
149	| `/help` | Show available commands and tools |
150	| `/model [name]` | Switch model or list available models |
151	| `/compact` | Compress conversation context to save tokens |
152	| `/clear` | Clear chat display |
153	| `/cost` | Show token usage and cost breakdown |
154	| `/diff [args]` | Show git diff |
155	| `/config [key=val]` | View or update configuration |
156	| `/commit [msg]` | Create a git commit |
157	
158	## Multi-Model Support
159	
160	Switch between providers at any time:
161	
162	```
163	/model anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514
164	/model openai:gpt-4o
165	/model google:gemini-2.0-flash
166	/model ollama:llama3.1:8b
167	```
168	
169	### Supported Providers
170	
171	| Provider | Models | API Key Env Var |
172	|----------|--------|-----------------|
173	| Anthropic | Claude 4 Sonnet, 3.5 Sonnet, 3 Haiku, 3 Opus | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` |
174	| OpenAI | GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4 Turbo | `OPENAI_API_KEY` |
175	| Google | Gemini 2.0 Flash, 1.5 Pro, 1.5 Flash | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` |
176	| Ollama | Any locally installed model | `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` |
177	
178	## How LangGraph Enables Key Features
179	
180	### Checkpointing & Session Persistence
181	
182	LangGraph's checkpointer serializes the entire graph state after each node execution. This means:
183	- Sessions survive process restarts
184	- You can resume any previous conversation with `--resume <thread-id>`
185	- State is stored in SQLite for zero-configuration persistence
186	
187	### Human-in-the-Loop
188	
189	When a tool requires permission, the graph uses LangGraph's `interrupt` mechanism to:
190	1. Pause execution at the permission check node
191	2. Yield control back to the TUI
192	3. Display the permission prompt to the user
193	4. Resume the graph with the user's decision
194	
195	### Streaming
196	
197	LangGraph's `astream_events` provides fine-grained streaming:
198	- Token-by-token output from the LLM
199	- Real-time tool execution status

### 2. Launch the TUI

```bash
tui-agent
```

### 3. Or run in headless mode

```bash
tui-agent --no-tui --model gpt-4o
```

### Command-line options

```
tui-agent [options]

Options:
  -m, --model MODEL       Model to use (default: claude-sonnet-4-20250514)
  -p, --provider PROVIDER Provider (anthropic, openai, google, ollama)
  -r, --resume THREAD_ID  Resume a previous session
  -w, --workdir PATH      Working directory (default: current)
  --no-tui                Run in headless CLI mode
  -v, --version           Show version
```

## Configuration

Configuration is stored at `~/.tui-agent/config.json`:

```json
{
  "default_provider": "anthropic",
  "default_model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  "max_iterations": 50,
  "auto_approve_reads": true,
  "auto_approve_writes": false,
  "auto_approve_bash": false,
  "theme": "dark",
  "show_sidebar": true
}
```

API keys are read from environment variables and never persisted to disk.

## Tools

| Tool | Category | Description |
|------|----------|-------------|
| `bash` | Shell | Execute shell commands with timeout and working directory |
| `file_read` | File | Read file contents with line numbers, offset, and limit |
| `file_write` | File | Create or overwrite files, auto-creating directories |
| `file_edit` | File | Precise string replacement in files |
| `glob` | Search | Find files by glob pattern (e.g., `**/*.py`) |
| `grep` | Search | Regex content search with context lines |
| `web_fetch` | Web | Fetch and extract text from URLs |
| `web_search` | Web | Search the web via DuckDuckGo |

### Permission Tiers

- **Auto-approved**: `file_read`, `glob`, `grep`, `web_fetch`, `web_search`
- **Requires approval**: `bash`, `file_write`, `file_edit`
- **Always blocked**: Destructive commands (`rm -rf /`, `mkfs`, etc.)

## Slash Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show available commands and tools |
| `/model [name]` | Switch model or list available models |
| `/compact` | Compress conversation context to save tokens |
| `/clear` | Clear chat display |
| `/cost` | Show token usage and cost breakdown |
| `/diff [args]` | Show git diff |
| `/config [key=val]` | View or update configuration |
| `/commit [msg]` | Create a git commit |

## Multi-Model Support

Switch between providers at any time:

```
/model anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-20250514
/model openai:gpt-4o
/model google:gemini-2.0-flash
/model ollama:llama3.1:8b
```

### Supported Providers

| Provider | Models | API Key Env Var |
|----------|--------|-----------------|
| Anthropic | Claude 4 Sonnet, 3.5 Sonnet, 3 Haiku, 3 Opus | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` |
| OpenAI | GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini, GPT-4 Turbo | `OPENAI_API_KEY` |
| Google | Gemini 2.0 Flash, 1.5 Pro, 1.5 Flash | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` |
| Ollama | Any locally installed model | `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` |

## How LangGraph Enables Key Features

### Checkpointing & Session Persistence

LangGraph's checkpointer serializes the entire graph state after each node execution. This means:
- Sessions survive process restarts
- You can resume any previous conversation with `--resume <thread-id>`
- State is stored in SQLite for zero-configuration persistence

### Human-in-the-Loop

When a tool requires permission, the graph uses LangGraph's `interrupt` mechanism to:
1. Pause execution at the permission check node
2. Yield control back to the TUI
3. Display the permission prompt to the user
4. Resume the graph with the user's decision

### Streaming

LangGraph's `astream_events` provides fine-grained streaming:
- Token-by-token output from the LLM
- Real-time tool execution status

content.content:
80	|----------|----------|
81	| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Anthropic (Claude) |
82	| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | OpenAI (GPT) |
83	| `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | Google (Gemini) |
84	
85	### Config File
86	
87	Settings are persisted in `~/.tui-agent/config.json`:
88	
89	```json
90	{
91	  "provider": "anthropic",
92	  "model": "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
93	  "max_tokens": 8192,
94	  "temperature": 0.0,
95	  "auto_approve": false
96	}
97	```
98	
99	### Persistent Memory
100	
101	Create an `AGENT.md` file in your project root (or `~/.tui-agent/AGENT.md` for global notes) to give the agent persistent context about your project. This is loaded automatically at the start of each session.
102	
103	## Architecture
104	
105	```
106	┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
107	│                  Textual TUI                     │
108	│  ┌──────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌───────────┐ │
109	│  │ Sidebar  │  │  Chat Area   │  │ Status Bar│ │
110	│  │ (files)  │  │  (messages)  │  │ (tokens)  │ │
111	│  └──────────┘  └──────────────┘  └───────────┘ │
112	│                ┌──────────────┐                  │
113	│                │  User Input  │                  │
114	│                └──────┬───────┘                  │
115	└───────────────────────┼─────────────────────────┘
116	                        │
117	                ┌───────▼───────┐
118	                │ Command Router │ ← Slash commands
119	                └───────┬───────┘
120	                        │
121	                ┌───────▼───────┐
122	                │  AgentRunner   │
123	                │  (LangChain    │
124	                │  AgentExecutor)│
125	                └───────┬───────┘
126	                        │
127	            ┌───────────┼───────────┐
128	            │           │           │
129	    ┌───────▼──┐ ┌──────▼───┐ ┌────▼─────┐
130	    │ LLM      │ │  Tools   │ │ Memory   │
131	    │ Provider │ │ Registry │ │ Manager  │
132	    │ Registry │ │          │ │          │
133	    └──────────┘ └──────────┘ └──────────┘
134	```
135	
136	### Core Components
137	
138	- **`app.py`** - Main Textual application, composes UI and handles events
139	- **`agent/core.py`** - LangChain AgentExecutor setup with tool-calling agent
140	- **`agent/prompts.py`** - System prompt engineering for coding assistance
141	- **`agent/callbacks.py`** - Async streaming callbacks that bridge agent to TUI
142	- **`llm/registry.py`** - Multi-provider LLM factory
143	- **`tools/registry.py`** - Tool factory with working directory configuration
144	- **`commands/registry.py`** - Slash command parsing and dispatch
145	
146	## Tools
147	
148	| Tool | Description |
149	|------|-------------|
150	| `bash` | Execute shell commands with timeout and working directory |
151	| `file_read` | Read files with line numbers, offset/limit pagination |
152	| `file_write` | Create or overwrite files with parent directory creation |
153	| `file_edit` | Precise string replacement edits (find and replace) |
154	| `glob` | Find files by glob pattern (e.g., `**/*.py`) |
155	| `grep` | Search file contents with regex (uses ripgrep when available) |
156	| `web_fetch` | Fetch and extract text content from URLs |
157	| `web_search` | Search the web via DuckDuckGo |
158	
159	## Slash Commands
160	
161	| Command | Description |
162	|---------|-------------|
163	| `/help` | Show all commands and keyboard shortcuts |
164	| `/model [provider] [model]` | View or switch the active LLM |
165	| `/compact [n]` | Compress history, keeping last N exchanges |
166	| `/clear` | Clear conversation and start fresh |
167	| `/cost` | Show token usage and estimated cost |
168	| `/diff [--staged] [file]` | Show git diff |
169	| `/config [set key value \| save]` | View or modify settings |
170	| `/commit [message]` | Create a git commit |
171	
172	## Keyboard Shortcuts
173	
174	| Key | Action |
175	|-----|--------|
176	| `Enter` | Submit message |
177	| `Shift+Enter` | New line in input |
178	| `Ctrl+B` | Toggle file tree sidebar |
179	| `Ctrl+L` | Clear screen |
180	| `Ctrl+Q` | Quit |
181	| `Up/Down` | Navigate input history |
182	
183	## Supported Models
184	
185	### Anthropic
186	- claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (default)
187	- claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022
188	- claude-3-opus-20240229
189	- claude-3-haiku-20240307
190	
191	### OpenAI
192	- gpt-4o
193	- gpt-4o-mini
194	- gpt-4-turbo
195	- o1-preview / o1-mini
196	
197	### Google
198	- gemini-2.0-flash
199	- gemini-1.5-pro
200	- gemini-1.5-flash
201	
202	### Ollama (local)
203	- llama3.1 / llama3.1:70b
204	- codellama
205	- deepseek-coder-v2
206	- mixtral
207	- qwen2.5-coder
208	
209	## Development
210	
211	```bash
212	# Clone and install in development mode
213	git clone https://github.com/your-org/tui-code-agent-langchain.git
214	cd tui-code-agent-langchain
215	pip install -e ".[dev]"
216	
217	# Run tests
218	pytest
219	
220	# Lint
221	ruff check src/
222	ruff format src/
223	
224	# Type check
225	mypy src/
226	```
227	
228	## Contributing
229

### Persistent Memory

Create an `AGENT.md` file in your project root (or `~/.tui-agent/AGENT.md` for global notes) to give the agent persistent context about your project. This is loaded automatically at the start of each session.

## Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  Textual TUI                     │

                        │

            ┌───────────┼───────────┐

    ┌───────▼──┐ ┌──────▼───┐ ┌────▼─────┐
    │ LLM      │ │  Tools   │ │ Memory   │
    │ Provider │ │ Registry │ │ Manager  │
    │ Registry │ │          │ │          │

```

### Core Components

- **`app.py`** - Main Textual application, composes UI and handles events
- **`agent/core.py`** - LangChain AgentExecutor setup with tool-calling agent
- **`agent/prompts.py`** - System prompt engineering for coding assistance
- **`agent/callbacks.py`** - Async streaming callbacks that bridge agent to TUI
- **`llm/registry.py`** - Multi-provider LLM factory
- **`tools/registry.py`** - Tool factory with working directory configuration
- **`commands/registry.py`** - Slash command parsing and dispatch

## Tools

| Tool | Description |
|------|-------------|
| `bash` | Execute shell commands with timeout and working directory |
| `file_read` | Read files with line numbers, offset/limit pagination |
| `file_write` | Create or overwrite files with parent directory creation |
| `file_edit` | Precise string replacement edits (find and replace) |
| `glob` | Find files by glob pattern (e.g., `**/*.py`) |
| `grep` | Search file contents with regex (uses ripgrep when available) |
| `web_fetch` | Fetch and extract text content from URLs |
| `web_search` | Search the web via DuckDuckGo |

## Slash Commands

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `/help` | Show all commands and keyboard shortcuts |
| `/model [provider] [model]` | View or switch the active LLM |
| `/compact [n]` | Compress history, keeping last N exchanges |
| `/clear` | Clear conversation and start fresh |
| `/cost` | Show token usage and estimated cost |
| `/diff [--staged] [file]` | Show git diff |
| `/config [set key value \| save]` | View or modify settings |
| `/commit [message]` | Create a git commit |

## Keyboard Shortcuts

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `Enter` | Submit message |
| `Shift+Enter` | New line in input |
| `Ctrl+B` | Toggle file tree sidebar |
| `Ctrl+L` | Clear screen |
| `Ctrl+Q` | Quit |
| `Up/Down` | Navigate input history |

## Supported Models

### Anthropic

## 📊 项目完成总结

### 🔗 项目 1：TUI Code Agent (LangChain)
**位置**: `~/zql/zql/zql/tui-agent-langchain/`

| 指标 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| Python 文件 | 53 个 |
| 总文件数 | 57 个 |
| Python 代码行 | 4,646 行 |
| README | 260 行，包含完整文档 |

**核心架构**: LangChain `AgentExecutor` + `create_tool_calling_agent`
- `agent/core.py` - AgentExecutor 循环
- `agent/prompts.py` - 系统提示词
- `agent/callbacks.py` - 流式回调到 TUI

### 🕸️ 项目 2：TUI Code Agent (LangGraph)
**位置**: `~/zql/zql/zql/tui-agent-langgraph/`

| 指标 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| Python 文件 | 55 个 |
| 总文件数 | 59 个 |
| Python 代码行 | 4,158 行 |
| README | 302 行，包含 Mermaid 架构图 |

**核心架构**: LangGraph `StateGraph` (think → act → observe 循环)
- `graph/agent.py` - StateGraph 定义
- `graph/nodes.py` - think/act/observe/respond 节点
- `graph/edges.py` - 条件边路由
- `graph/state.py` - AgentState TypedDict
- `graph/checkpointer.py` - SQLite 持久化

| 会话历史管理 | ✅ |
| Git 集成 | ✅ |
| pyproject.toml + MIT License | ✅ |
| 完整 README 文档 | ✅ |
