"""Main Textual TUI application."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import logging
from typing import Any

from textual.app import App, ComposeResult
from textual.binding import Binding
from textual.containers import Horizontal, Vertical
from textual.widgets import Footer, Header

from tui_agent.commands.registry import get_command_registry
from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.memory.conversation import ConversationManager
from tui_agent.ui.chat import ChatDisplay
from tui_agent.ui.input import UserInput
from tui_agent.ui.sidebar import FileTreeSidebar
from tui_agent.ui.status import StatusBar
from tui_agent.utils.cost import format_cost_summary

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class TUIAgentApp(App):
    """Main TUI application for the coding agent.

    Integrates the LangGraph agent with a Textual-based terminal UI
    featuring chat display, input, sidebar, and status bar.
    """

    TITLE = "TUI Code Agent"
    SUB_TITLE = "LangGraph-powered coding assistant"
    CSS = """
    Screen {
        background: #1e1e2e;
    }
    #main-container {
        height: 1fr;
    }
    #chat-area {
        width: 1fr;
    }
    """

    BINDINGS = [
        Binding("ctrl+c", "quit", "Quit", priority=True),
        Binding("ctrl+l", "clear_screen", "Clear"),
        Binding("tab", "toggle_sidebar", "Toggle Sidebar"),
    ]

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: Config | None = None,
        resume_thread: str | None = None,
        **kwargs: Any,
    ) -> None:
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.config = config or Config.load()
        self._conversation = ConversationManager()
        self._graph: Any = None
        self._checkpointer: Any = None
        self._sidebar_visible = self.config.show_sidebar
        self._is_processing = False

        # State tracking
        self._total_tokens = 0
        self._total_cost = 0.0
        self._current_model = self.config.default_model
        self._current_provider = self.config.default_provider

        if resume_thread:
            self._conversation.resume_thread(resume_thread)

    def compose(self) -> ComposeResult:
        """Compose the application layout."""
        yield Header()
        with Horizontal(id="main-container"):
            if self._sidebar_visible:
                yield FileTreeSidebar(
                    working_directory=self.config.working_directory,
                    id="sidebar",
                )
            with Vertical(id="chat-area"):
                yield ChatDisplay(id="chat-display")
                yield UserInput(id="user-input")
        yield StatusBar(id="status-bar")
        yield Footer()

    async def on_mount(self) -> None:
        """Initialize the agent graph on mount."""
        from tui_agent.graph.agent import create_agent_graph
        from tui_agent.graph.checkpointer import create_checkpointer

        self._checkpointer = await create_checkpointer(self.config.db_path)
        self._graph = create_agent_graph(self.config, self._checkpointer)

        # Update status bar
        status = self.query_one("#status-bar", StatusBar)
        status.update_from_state(
            model=self._current_model,
            provider=self._current_provider,
            working_dir=self.config.working_directory,
            status="Ready",
        )

        # Welcome message
        chat = self.query_one("#chat-display", ChatDisplay)
        chat.add_assistant_message(
            "Welcome to **TUI Code Agent**! I'm a coding assistant powered by "
            f"LangGraph.\n\n"
            f"**Model**: {self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}\n"
            f"**Working directory**: {self.config.working_directory}\n\n"
            "Type a message or use `/help` for available commands."
        )

        # Focus input
        self.query_one("#user-input", UserInput).focus()

    async def on_user_input_submitted(self, event: UserInput.Submitted) -> None:
        """Handle user input submission."""
        text = event.value.strip()
        if not text:
            return

        chat = self.query_one("#chat-display", ChatDisplay)

        if event.is_command:
            # Handle slash command
            chat.add_user_message(text)
            result = await self._handle_command(text)
            chat.add_assistant_message(result)
            return

        if self._is_processing:
            chat.add_error_message("Still processing previous request. Please wait.")
            return

        # Send to agent
        chat.add_user_message(text)
        self._is_processing = True

        status = self.query_one("#status-bar", StatusBar)
        status.set_status("Thinking...")

        # Run agent in background task
        self.run_worker(self._run_agent(text), exclusive=True)

    async def _run_agent(self, user_input: str) -> None:
        """Run the agent graph with user input.

        Args:
            user_input: User's message text.
        """
        from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, ToolMessage

        chat = self.query_one("#chat-display", ChatDisplay)
        status = self.query_one("#status-bar", StatusBar)

        try:
            graph_config = self._conversation.get_config()
            inputs = {
                "messages": [HumanMessage(content=user_input)],
                "current_model": self._current_model,
                "current_provider": self._current_provider,
                "working_directory": self.config.working_directory,
            }

            # Stream events from the graph
            streaming_widget = None
            last_ai_content = ""

            async for event in self._graph.astream_events(
                inputs, config=graph_config, version="v2"
            ):
                kind = event.get("event", "")

                if kind == "on_chat_model_stream":
                    chunk = event.get("data", {}).get("chunk")
                    if chunk and hasattr(chunk, "content") and isinstance(chunk.content, str):
                        if streaming_widget is None:
                            streaming_widget = chat.start_streaming()
                        streaming_widget.append_token(chunk.content)
                        self.call_later(lambda: chat.scroll_end(animate=False))

                elif kind == "on_chain_end":
                    output = event.get("data", {}).get("output", {})
                    if isinstance(output, dict):
                        # Update token/cost tracking
                        if "total_tokens" in output:
                            self._total_tokens = output["total_tokens"]
                            status.set_tokens(self._total_tokens)
                        if "total_cost" in output:
                            self._total_cost = output["total_cost"]
                            status.set_cost(self._total_cost)

                        # Show tool results
                        messages = output.get("messages", [])
                        for msg in messages:
                            if isinstance(msg, ToolMessage):
                                tool_name = getattr(msg, "name", "tool")
                                chat.add_tool_message(tool_name, msg.content)

                        # Finalize streaming if we had one
                        if streaming_widget is not None:
                            last_ai_content = streaming_widget.finalize()
                            streaming_widget = None

            # If no streaming happened, check for final AI message
            if not last_ai_content and streaming_widget:
                last_ai_content = streaming_widget.finalize()

            self._conversation.increment_turn()

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error("Agent error: %s", e, exc_info=True)
            chat.add_error_message(f"Agent error: {e}")
        finally:
            self._is_processing = False
            status.set_status("Ready")

    async def _handle_command(self, command_text: str) -> str:
        """Handle a slash command.

        Args:
            command_text: Full command string including leading slash.

        Returns:
            Command result text.
        """
        registry = get_command_registry()
        return await registry.execute(command_text, self)

    # Command context methods (called by slash commands)

    def set_model(self, provider: str, model: str) -> None:
        """Set the active model (called by /model command)."""
        self._current_provider = provider
        self._current_model = model
        status = self.query_one("#status-bar", StatusBar)
        status.set_model(provider, model)

    def clear_chat(self) -> None:
        """Clear the chat display (called by /clear command)."""
        chat = self.query_one("#chat-display", ChatDisplay)
        chat.clear_messages()

    @property
    def working_directory(self) -> str:
        """Current working directory."""
        return self.config.working_directory

    def get_cost_summary(self) -> str:
        """Get formatted cost summary (called by /cost command)."""
        return format_cost_summary(
            model=self._current_model,
            total_tokens=self._total_tokens,
            total_cost=self._total_cost,
        )

    def action_toggle_sidebar(self) -> None:
        """Toggle the file tree sidebar visibility."""
        sidebar = self.query_one("#sidebar", FileTreeSidebar)
        self._sidebar_visible = not self._sidebar_visible
        sidebar.display = self._sidebar_visible

    def action_clear_screen(self) -> None:
        """Clear the chat display."""
        self.clear_chat()

"""Configuration management for TUI Agent."""

from __future__ import annotations

import json
import os
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any


DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR = Path.home() / ".tui-agent"
DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "config.json"
DEFAULT_DB_PATH = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "sessions.db"
DEFAULT_MEMORY_DIR = DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR / "memory"


@dataclass
class Config:
    """Application configuration."""

    default_provider: str = "anthropic"
    default_model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514"
    working_directory: str = field(default_factory=lambda: os.getcwd())
    max_iterations: int = 50
    max_tokens_per_response: int = 8192
    temperature: float = 0.0
    config_dir: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(DEFAULT_CONFIG_DIR))
    db_path: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(DEFAULT_DB_PATH))
    memory_dir: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(DEFAULT_MEMORY_DIR))

    # Permission settings
    auto_approve_reads: bool = True
    auto_approve_writes: bool = False
    auto_approve_bash: bool = False
    allowed_bash_commands: list[str] = field(
        default_factory=lambda: [
            "git", "ls", "find", "grep", "rg", "cat", "head", "tail",
            "wc", "sort", "uniq", "diff", "echo", "pwd", "which", "env",
            "python", "pip", "node", "npm", "cargo", "go", "make",
        ]
    )

    # UI settings
    theme: str = "dark"
    show_sidebar: bool = True
    show_status_bar: bool = True
    show_token_count: bool = True
    show_cost: bool = True
    sidebar_width: int = 30

    # Provider API keys (loaded from env if not set)
    anthropic_api_key: str = ""
    openai_api_key: str = ""
    google_api_key: str = ""
    ollama_base_url: str = "http://localhost:11434"

    # Model pricing (per 1M tokens: [input, output])
    model_pricing: dict[str, list[float]] = field(default_factory=lambda: {
        "claude-sonnet-4-20250514": [3.0, 15.0],
        "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022": [3.0, 15.0],
        "claude-3-haiku-20240307": [0.25, 1.25],
        "gpt-4o": [2.50, 10.0],
        "gpt-4o-mini": [0.15, 0.60],
        "gpt-4-turbo": [10.0, 30.0],
        "gemini-2.0-flash": [0.075, 0.30],
        "gemini-1.5-pro": [1.25, 5.0],
    })

    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
        """Load API keys from environment if not already set."""
        if not self.anthropic_api_key:
            self.anthropic_api_key = os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")
        if not self.openai_api_key:
            self.openai_api_key = os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY", "")
        if not self.google_api_key:
            self.google_api_key = os.environ.get("GOOGLE_API_KEY", "")
        if not self.ollama_base_url:
            self.ollama_base_url = os.environ.get("OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:11434")

    @classmethod
    def load(cls, path: str | Path | None = None) -> Config:
        """Load configuration from a JSON file, falling back to defaults."""
        config_path = Path(path) if path else DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE
        data: dict[str, Any] = {}

        if config_path.exists():
            try:
                data = json.loads(config_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
            except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):
                pass

        # Set API keys and base URLs from config into env vars
        _ENV_KEYS = [
            "ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL",
            "OPENAI_API_KEY", "OPENAI_BASE_URL",
            "GOOGLE_API_KEY",
            "OLLAMA_BASE_URL",
        ]
        for env_key in _ENV_KEYS:
            if env_key in data and data[env_key] and not os.environ.get(env_key):
                os.environ[env_key] = str(data[env_key])

        # Filter to only known fields
        valid_fields = {f.name for f in cls.__dataclass_fields__.values()}
        filtered = {k: v for k, v in data.items() if k in valid_fields}
        return cls(**filtered)

    def save(self, path: str | Path | None = None) -> None:
        """Save configuration to a JSON file."""
        config_path = Path(path) if path else DEFAULT_CONFIG_FILE
        config_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

        data = {
            k: v for k, v in self.__dict__.items()
            if not k.endswith("_api_key")  # Never persist API keys
        }
        config_path.write_text(
            json.dumps(data, indent=2, default=str),
            encoding="utf-8",
        )

    def get_api_key(self, provider: str) -> str:
        """Get API key for a provider."""
        key_map = {
            "anthropic": self.anthropic_api_key,
            "openai": self.openai_api_key,
            "google": self.google_api_key,
        }
        return key_map.get(provider, "")

    def get_pricing(self, model: str) -> tuple[float, float]:
        """Get pricing for a model as (input_per_1m, output_per_1m)."""
        pricing = self.model_pricing.get(model, [0.0, 0.0])
        return (pricing[0], pricing[1])

1	"""Main agent StateGraph definition."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import logging
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from langgraph.checkpoint.base import BaseCheckpointSaver
9	from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph
10	
11	from tui_agent.config import Config
12	from tui_agent.graph.edges import after_observe, check_permissions_edge, should_use_tools
13	from tui_agent.graph.nodes import act_node, observe_node, respond_node, think_node
14	from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState
15	
16	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
17	
18	
19	async def check_permissions_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
20	    """Check permissions for pending tool calls.
21	
22	    Evaluates each tool call against the permission policy. Read-only tools
23	    (file_read, glob, grep) are auto-approved. Write/execute tools require
24	    user confirmation unless explicitly allowed in config.
25	
26	    For now, this node auto-approves all calls. The permission manager
27	    integration provides the actual gating logic via the TUI.

"""Main agent StateGraph definition."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any

from langgraph.checkpoint.base import BaseCheckpointSaver
from langgraph.graph import END, StateGraph

from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.graph.edges import after_observe, check_permissions_edge, should_use_tools
from tui_agent.graph.nodes import act_node, observe_node, respond_node, think_node
from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def check_permissions_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Check permissions for pending tool calls.

    Evaluates each tool call against the permission policy. Read-only tools
    (file_read, glob, grep) are auto-approved. Write/execute tools require
    user confirmation unless explicitly allowed in config.

    For now, this node auto-approves all calls. The permission manager
    integration provides the actual gating logic via the TUI.
    """
    from tui_agent.permissions.manager import PermissionManager
    from tui_agent.tools.registry import get_tool_registry

    tool_calls = state.get("tool_calls", [])
    if not tool_calls:
        return {"permission_pending": None}

    manager = PermissionManager()
    tool_reg = get_tool_registry()

    for tc in tool_calls:
        tool_name = tc.get("name", "")
        tool = tool_reg.get_tool(tool_name)
        if tool is None:
            continue

        is_read_only = getattr(tool, "read_only", False)
        if is_read_only:
            continue

        if not manager.is_auto_approved(tool_name, tc.get("args", {})):
            # In a full implementation, this would use LangGraph's interrupt()
            # to pause and wait for user input via the TUI.
            # For now we auto-approve to keep the graph flowing.
            logger.info("Auto-approving tool call: %s", tool_name)

    return {"permission_pending": None}


async def wait_for_permission_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Wait for user to approve or deny a permission request.

    In production, this uses LangGraph's interrupt mechanism to pause
    the graph and yield control back to the TUI for user input.
    """
    # This would use: from langgraph.types import interrupt
    # result = interrupt(state["permission_pending"])
    # For now, auto-approve
    return {"permission_pending": None}


def create_agent_graph(
    config: Config,
    checkpointer: BaseCheckpointSaver | None = None,
) -> Any:
    """Create and compile the agent StateGraph.

    Graph topology:
        START -> think -> should_use_tools?
          -> YES -> check_permissions -> check_permissions_edge?
              -> act (approved) -> observe -> after_observe?
                  -> think (continue loop)
                  -> respond (max iterations)
              -> wait_for_permission (needs approval) -> act -> ...
          -> NO -> respond -> END

    Args:
        config: Application configuration.
        checkpointer: Optional checkpointer for state persistence.

    Returns:
        Compiled LangGraph StateGraph ready for invocation.
    """
    # Build the graph
    graph = StateGraph(AgentState)

    # Add nodes
    graph.add_node("think", think_node)
    graph.add_node("check_permissions", check_permissions_node)
    graph.add_node("wait_for_permission", wait_for_permission_node)
    graph.add_node("act", act_node)
    graph.add_node("observe", observe_node)
    graph.add_node("respond", respond_node)

    # Set entry point
    graph.set_entry_point("think")

    # Add edges
    # After think: decide if we need tools or can respond
    graph.add_conditional_edges(
        "think",
        should_use_tools,
        {
            "check_permissions": "check_permissions",
            "respond": "respond",
        },
    )

    # After permission check: either act or wait
    graph.add_conditional_edges(
        "check_permissions",
        check_permissions_edge,
        {
            "act": "act",
            "wait_for_permission": "wait_for_permission",
        },
    )

    # After waiting for permission: proceed to act
    graph.add_edge("wait_for_permission", "act")

    # After act: observe results
    graph.add_edge("act", "observe")

    # After observe: loop back to think or respond
    graph.add_conditional_edges(
        "observe",
        after_observe,
        {
            "think": "think",
            "respond": "respond",
        },
    )

    # Respond is the terminal node
    graph.add_edge("respond", END)

    # Compile with checkpointer
    compiled = graph.compile(checkpointer=checkpointer)
    logger.info("Agent graph compiled successfully")
    return compiled

"""Agent state definition for the LangGraph StateGraph."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Annotated, Any, Optional

from langchain_core.messages import BaseMessage
from langgraph.graph.message import add_messages
from typing_extensions import TypedDict


class AgentState(TypedDict):
    """State flowing through the agent graph.

    Attributes:
        messages: Conversation message history (auto-merged via add_messages reducer).
        tool_calls: Pending tool calls from the LLM response.
        tool_results: Results from executed tools, fed back to the LLM.
        current_model: Active model identifier (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514').
        current_provider: Active provider name (e.g. 'anthropic').
        working_directory: Current working directory for file/shell operations.
        permission_pending: Tool call awaiting user permission approval, or None.
        iteration_count: Number of think-act-observe loops in the current turn.
        total_tokens: Cumulative token usage across the session.
        total_cost: Cumulative cost in USD across the session.
        turn_tokens: Token usage for the current turn only.
        turn_cost: Cost for the current turn only.
        error: Last error message if any, cleared on next successful step.
        metadata: Arbitrary metadata for extensibility.
    """

    messages: Annotated[list[BaseMessage], add_messages]
    tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]]
    tool_results: list[dict[str, Any]]
    current_model: str
    current_provider: str
    working_directory: str
    permission_pending: Optional[dict[str, Any]]
    iteration_count: int
    total_tokens: int
    total_cost: float
    turn_tokens: int
    turn_cost: float
    error: Optional[str]
    metadata: dict[str, Any]


def create_initial_state(
    model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
    provider: str = "anthropic",
    working_directory: str = ".",
) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Create the initial state dictionary for a new agent session.

    Args:
        model: Default model identifier.
        provider: Default provider name.
        working_directory: Starting working directory.

    Returns:
        Dictionary matching AgentState schema with sensible defaults.
    """
    return {
        "messages": [],
        "tool_calls": [],
        "tool_results": [],
        "current_model": model,
        "current_provider": provider,
        "working_directory": working_directory,
        "permission_pending": None,
        "iteration_count": 0,
        "total_tokens": 0,
        "total_cost": 0.0,
        "turn_tokens": 0,
        "turn_cost": 0.0,
        "error": None,
        "metadata": {},
    }

1	"""Graph nodes: think, act, observe, respond."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import logging
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage, ToolMessage
9	
10	from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState
11	from tui_agent.llm.registry import get_provider_registry
12	from tui_agent.tools.registry import get_tool_registry
13	from tui_agent.utils.cost import calculate_cost
14	from tui_agent.utils.tokens import count_message_tokens
15	
16	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
17	
18	SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
19	You are an expert coding assistant running inside a terminal TUI. You help users \
20	with software development tasks by reading, writing, and editing files, running \
21	shell commands, searching code, and more.
22	
23	Guidelines:
24	- Be concise and direct in your responses.
25	- When modifying files, show what you changed and why.
26	- Use tools to gather information before making changes.
27	- Ask for clarification when requirements are ambiguous.
28	- Prefer safe, non-destructive operations.
29	- When running shell commands, explain what they do.
30	- Current working directory: {working_directory}
31	"""
32	
33	
34	async def think_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
35	    """Call the LLM with the current messages and available tool schemas.
36	
37	    This is the core reasoning node. It sends the conversation history
38	    plus tool definitions to the LLM and receives a response that may
39	    contain text, tool calls, or both.
40	
41	    Returns:
42	        State update with new AI message, any tool_calls extracted,
43	        and updated token/cost counters.
44	    """
45	    provider_name = state.get("current_provider", "anthropic")
46	    model_name = state.get("current_model", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514")
47	    working_dir = state.get("working_directory", ".")
48	
49	    registry = get_provider_registry()
50	    provider = registry.get_provider(provider_name)
51	    if provider is None:
52	        return {
53	            "error": f"Provider '{provider_name}' not available. Check API key.",
54	            "tool_calls": [],
55	        }
56	
57	    llm = provider.get_chat_model(model_name)
58	
59	    # Bind tools
60	    tool_registry = get_tool_registry()
61	    tools = tool_registry.get_langchain_tools()
62	    if tools:
63	        llm = llm.bind_tools(tools)
64	
65	    # Build messages with system prompt
66	    system_msg = SystemMessage(content=SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(working_directory=working_dir))
67	    messages = [system_msg] + list(state.get("messages", []))
68	
69	    try:
70	        response: AIMessage = await llm.ainvoke(messages)
71	    except Exception as e:
72	        logger.error("LLM invocation failed: %s", e)
73	        return {
74	            "messages": [AIMessage(content=f"Error calling LLM: {e}")],
75	            "tool_calls": [],
76	            "error": str(e),
77	        }
78	
79	    # Extract tool calls
80	    tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
81	    if hasattr(response, "tool_calls") and response.tool_calls:
82	        for tc in response.tool_calls:
83	            tool_calls.append({
84	                "id": tc.get("id", ""),
85	                "name": tc.get("name", ""),
86	                "args": tc.get("args", {}),
87	            })
88	
89	    # Token counting
90	    input_tokens = count_message_tokens(messages, model_name)
91	    output_tokens = count_message_tokens([response], model_name)
92	    total_turn_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
93	    cost = calculate_cost(model_name, input_tokens, output_tokens)
94	
95	    return {
96	        "messages": [response],
97	        "tool_calls": tool_calls,
98	        "iteration_count": state.get("iteration_count", 0) + 1,
99	        "turn_tokens": state.get("turn_tokens", 0) + total_turn_tokens,
100	        "turn_cost": state.get("turn_cost", 0.0) + cost,
101	        "total_tokens": state.get("total_tokens", 0) + total_turn_tokens,
102	        "total_cost": state.get("total_cost", 0.0) + cost,
103	        "error": None,
104	    }
105	
106	
107	async def act_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
108	    """Execute pending tool calls.
109	
110	    Iterates over each tool call in state, invokes the corresponding
111	    tool, and collects results.
112	
113	    Returns:
114	        State update with tool_results populated and tool_calls cleared.
115	    """
116	    tool_calls = state.get("tool_calls", [])
117	    if not tool_calls:
118	        return {"tool_results": [], "tool_calls": []}
119	
120	    tool_registry = get_tool_registry()
121	    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
122	
123	    for tc in tool_calls:
124	        tool_name = tc.get("name", "")
125	        tool_args = tc.get("args", {})
126	        tool_id = tc.get("id", "")
127	
128	        tool = tool_registry.get_tool(tool_name)
129	        if tool is None:
130	            results.append({
131	                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
132	                "name": tool_name,
133	                "content": f"Error: Tool '{tool_name}' not found.",
134	                "is_error": True,
135	            })
136	            continue
137	
138	        # Inject working directory for tools that need it
139	        working_dir = state.get("working_directory", ".")
140	        if "working_directory" not in tool_args and hasattr(tool, "requires_working_dir"):
141	            tool_args["working_directory"] = working_dir
142	
143	        try:
144	            result = await tool.ainvoke(tool_args)
145	            content = str(result) if result is not None else "Done (no output)."
146	            # Truncate very large outputs
147	            if len(content) > 50_000:
148	                content = content[:50_000] + "\n... [output truncated at 50,000 chars]"
149	            results.append({
150	                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
151	                "name": tool_name,
152	                "content": content,
153	                "is_error": False,
154	            })
155	        except Exception as e:
156	            logger.error("Tool '%s' failed: %s", tool_name, e)
157	            results.append({
158	                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
159	                "name": tool_name,
160	                "content": f"Error executing {tool_name}: {e}",
161	                "is_error": True,
162	            })
163	
164	    return {"tool_results": results, "tool_calls": []}
165	
166	
167	async def observe_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
168	    """Convert tool results into ToolMessages for the conversation.
169	
170	    Takes the raw tool_results from the act node and creates proper
171	    LangChain ToolMessage objects that the LLM can process.
172	
173	    Returns:
174	        State update with new ToolMessages added to messages, tool_results cleared.
175	    """
176	    tool_results = state.get("tool_results", [])
177	    if not tool_results:
178	        return {"messages": [], "tool_results": []}
179	
180	    messages: list[ToolMessage] = []
181	    for result in tool_results:
182	        messages.append(
183	            ToolMessage(
184	                content=result["content"],
185	                tool_call_id=result["tool_call_id"],
186	                name=result["name"],
187	            )
188	        )
189	
190	    return {"messages": messages, "tool_results": []}
191	
192	
193	async def respond_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
194	    """Final node: reset per-turn counters for the next user turn.
195	
196	    Returns:
197	        State update resetting iteration_count and turn counters.
198	    """
199	    return {
200	        "iteration_count": 0,
201	        "turn_tokens": 0,
202	        "turn_cost": 0.0,
203	        "error": None,
204	    }
205

"""Graph nodes: think, act, observe, respond."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any

from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, SystemMessage, ToolMessage

from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState
from tui_agent.llm.registry import get_provider_registry
from tui_agent.tools.registry import get_tool_registry
from tui_agent.utils.cost import calculate_cost
from tui_agent.utils.tokens import count_message_tokens

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

SYSTEM_PROMPT = """\
You are an expert coding assistant running inside a terminal TUI. You help users \
with software development tasks by reading, writing, and editing files, running \
shell commands, searching code, and more.

Guidelines:
- Be concise and direct in your responses.
- When modifying files, show what you changed and why.
- Use tools to gather information before making changes.
- Ask for clarification when requirements are ambiguous.
- Prefer safe, non-destructive operations.
- When running shell commands, explain what they do.
- Current working directory: {working_directory}
"""


async def think_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Call the LLM with the current messages and available tool schemas.

    This is the core reasoning node. It sends the conversation history
    plus tool definitions to the LLM and receives a response that may
    contain text, tool calls, or both.

    Returns:
        State update with new AI message, any tool_calls extracted,
        and updated token/cost counters.
    """
    provider_name = state.get("current_provider", "anthropic")
    model_name = state.get("current_model", "claude-sonnet-4-20250514")
    working_dir = state.get("working_directory", ".")

    registry = get_provider_registry()
    provider = registry.get_provider(provider_name)
    if provider is None:
        return {
            "error": f"Provider '{provider_name}' not available. Check API key.",
            "tool_calls": [],
        }

    llm = provider.get_chat_model(model_name)

    # Bind tools
    tool_registry = get_tool_registry()
    tools = tool_registry.get_langchain_tools()
    if tools:
        llm = llm.bind_tools(tools)

    # Build messages with system prompt
    system_msg = SystemMessage(content=SYSTEM_PROMPT.format(working_directory=working_dir))
    messages = [system_msg] + list(state.get("messages", []))

    try:
        response: AIMessage = await llm.ainvoke(messages)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error("LLM invocation failed: %s", e)
        return {
            "messages": [AIMessage(content=f"Error calling LLM: {e}")],
            "tool_calls": [],
            "error": str(e),
        }

    # Extract tool calls
    tool_calls: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
    if hasattr(response, "tool_calls") and response.tool_calls:
        for tc in response.tool_calls:
            tool_calls.append({
                "id": tc.get("id", ""),
                "name": tc.get("name", ""),
                "args": tc.get("args", {}),
            })

    # Token counting
    input_tokens = count_message_tokens(messages, model_name)
    output_tokens = count_message_tokens([response], model_name)
    total_turn_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens
    cost = calculate_cost(model_name, input_tokens, output_tokens)

    return {
        "messages": [response],
        "tool_calls": tool_calls,
        "iteration_count": state.get("iteration_count", 0) + 1,
        "turn_tokens": state.get("turn_tokens", 0) + total_turn_tokens,
        "turn_cost": state.get("turn_cost", 0.0) + cost,
        "total_tokens": state.get("total_tokens", 0) + total_turn_tokens,
        "total_cost": state.get("total_cost", 0.0) + cost,
        "error": None,
    }


async def act_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Execute pending tool calls.

    Iterates over each tool call in state, invokes the corresponding
    tool, and collects results.

    Returns:
        State update with tool_results populated and tool_calls cleared.
    """
    tool_calls = state.get("tool_calls", [])
    if not tool_calls:
        return {"tool_results": [], "tool_calls": []}

    tool_registry = get_tool_registry()
    results: list[dict[str, Any]] = []

    for tc in tool_calls:
        tool_name = tc.get("name", "")
        tool_args = tc.get("args", {})
        tool_id = tc.get("id", "")

        tool = tool_registry.get_tool(tool_name)
        if tool is None:
            results.append({
                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
                "name": tool_name,
                "content": f"Error: Tool '{tool_name}' not found.",
                "is_error": True,
            })
            continue

        # Inject working directory for tools that need it
        working_dir = state.get("working_directory", ".")
        if "working_directory" not in tool_args and hasattr(tool, "requires_working_dir"):
            tool_args["working_directory"] = working_dir

        try:
            result = await tool.ainvoke(tool_args)
            content = str(result) if result is not None else "Done (no output)."
            # Truncate very large outputs
            if len(content) > 50_000:
                content = content[:50_000] + "\n... [output truncated at 50,000 chars]"
            results.append({
                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
                "name": tool_name,
                "content": content,
                "is_error": False,
            })
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error("Tool '%s' failed: %s", tool_name, e)
            results.append({
                "tool_call_id": tool_id,
                "name": tool_name,
                "content": f"Error executing {tool_name}: {e}",
                "is_error": True,
            })

    return {"tool_results": results, "tool_calls": []}


async def observe_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Convert tool results into ToolMessages for the conversation.

    Takes the raw tool_results from the act node and creates proper
    LangChain ToolMessage objects that the LLM can process.

    Returns:
        State update with new ToolMessages added to messages, tool_results cleared.
    """
    tool_results = state.get("tool_results", [])
    if not tool_results:
        return {"messages": [], "tool_results": []}

    messages: list[ToolMessage] = []
    for result in tool_results:
        messages.append(
            ToolMessage(
                content=result["content"],
                tool_call_id=result["tool_call_id"],
                name=result["name"],
            )
        )

    return {"messages": messages, "tool_results": []}


async def respond_node(state: AgentState) -> dict[str, Any]:
    """Final node: reset per-turn counters for the next user turn.

    Returns:
        State update resetting iteration_count and turn counters.
    """
    return {
        "iteration_count": 0,
        "turn_tokens": 0,
        "turn_cost": 0.0,
        "error": None,
    }

"""Conditional edges for the agent graph."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Literal

from tui_agent.graph.state import AgentState

MAX_ITERATIONS = 50


def should_use_tools(state: AgentState) -> Literal["check_permissions", "respond"]:
    """Decide whether the LLM wants to use tools or respond directly.

    After the think node, check if the LLM produced any tool calls.
    If yes, route to permission checking. If no, route to respond.

    Args:
        state: Current agent state.

    Returns:
        Next node name: 'check_permissions' or 'respond'.
    """
    tool_calls = state.get("tool_calls", [])
    iteration_count = state.get("iteration_count", 0)

    # Safety: prevent infinite loops
    if iteration_count >= MAX_ITERATIONS:
        return "respond"

    if tool_calls:
        return "check_permissions"

    return "respond"


def check_permissions_edge(state: AgentState) -> Literal["act", "wait_for_permission"]:
    """Check if all pending tool calls are auto-approved.

    Examines each tool call and determines whether it requires explicit
    user permission. Read-only tools are typically auto-approved.

    Args:
        state: Current agent state.

    Returns:
        Next node name: 'act' if all approved, 'wait_for_permission' if any need approval.
    """
    permission_pending = state.get("permission_pending")
    if permission_pending is not None:
        # There's a pending permission request - wait
        return "wait_for_permission"

    # All permissions resolved
    return "act"


def after_observe(state: AgentState) -> Literal["think", "respond"]:
    """After observing tool results, decide whether to continue the loop.

    Routes back to think if there are more steps to take, or to respond
    if we've hit the iteration limit.

    Args:
        state: Current agent state.

    Returns:
        Next node name: 'think' to continue loop, 'respond' to finish.
    """
    iteration_count = state.get("iteration_count", 0)

    if iteration_count >= MAX_ITERATIONS:
        return "respond"

    return "think"

"""SQLite checkpointer for session persistence."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from pathlib import Path

from langgraph.checkpoint.memory import MemorySaver

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


async def create_checkpointer(
    db_path: str | Path | None = None,
) -> MemorySaver:
    """Create a checkpointer for graph state persistence.

    Uses MemorySaver as the default checkpointer. For production use
    with persistence across restarts, this can be swapped to
    langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite or langgraph-checkpoint-postgres.

    Args:
        db_path: Path to SQLite database file. Currently unused with
                 MemorySaver but reserved for future SQLite backend.

    Returns:
        A configured checkpointer instance.
    """
    # MemorySaver works in-process and is the simplest option.
    # For true persistence, install langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite and use:
    #   from langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite.aio import AsyncSqliteSaver
    #   return AsyncSqliteSaver.from_conn_string(str(db_path))
    logger.info("Using in-memory checkpointer (MemorySaver)")
    return MemorySaver()


def get_thread_config(thread_id: str) -> dict:
    """Build a LangGraph config dict with the given thread ID.

    Args:
        thread_id: Unique session/thread identifier.

    Returns:
        Config dict suitable for passing to graph.ainvoke / graph.astream_events.
    """
    return {"configurable": {"thread_id": thread_id}}

"""Provider registry for managing multiple LLM providers."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import os
from typing import Optional

from tui_agent.llm.provider import LLMProvider

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_registry: Optional[ProviderRegistry] = None


class ProviderRegistry:
    """Registry that manages all available LLM providers.

    Lazily initializes providers and caches them. Provides lookup
    by provider name and model discovery across all providers.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._providers: dict[str, LLMProvider] = {}
        self._initialized = False

    def _ensure_initialized(self) -> None:
        """Lazily initialize all built-in providers."""
        if self._initialized:
            return
        self._initialized = True

        from tui_agent.llm.anthropic import AnthropicProvider
        from tui_agent.llm.google import GoogleProvider
        from tui_agent.llm.ollama import OllamaProvider
        from tui_agent.llm.openai import OpenAIProvider

        providers: list[LLMProvider] = [
            AnthropicProvider(api_key=os.environ.get("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "")),
            OpenAIProvider(api_key=os.environ.get("OPENAI_API_KEY", "")),
            GoogleProvider(api_key=os.environ.get("GOOGLE_API_KEY", "")),
            OllamaProvider(base_url=os.environ.get("OLLAMA_BASE_URL", "")),
        ]

        for p in providers:
            self._providers[p.name] = p
            if p.is_available():
                logger.info("Provider '%s' is available", p.name)
            else:
                logger.debug("Provider '%s' is not configured", p.name)

    def register(self, provider: LLMProvider) -> None:
        """Register a custom provider.

        Args:
            provider: Provider instance to register.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        self._providers[provider.name] = provider
        logger.info("Registered custom provider: %s", provider.name)

    def get_provider(self, name: str) -> LLMProvider | None:
        """Get a provider by name.

        Args:
            name: Provider identifier.

        Returns:
            Provider instance or None if not found.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return self._providers.get(name)

    def list_providers(self) -> list[LLMProvider]:
        """List all registered providers.

        Returns:
            List of all provider instances.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return list(self._providers.values())

    def list_available_providers(self) -> list[LLMProvider]:
        """List only providers that are currently available/configured.

        Returns:
            List of available provider instances.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return [p for p in self._providers.values() if p.is_available()]

    def list_all_models(self) -> dict[str, list[str]]:
        """List all models grouped by provider.

        Returns:
            Dict mapping provider name to list of model identifiers.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        result: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
        for p in self._providers.values():
            if p.is_available():
                result[p.name] = p.list_models()
        return result

    def find_provider_for_model(self, model: str) -> LLMProvider | None:
        """Find which provider serves a given model.

        Args:
            model: Model identifier to look up.

        Returns:
            Provider that serves the model, or None.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        for p in self._providers.values():
            if model in p.list_models() and p.is_available():
                return p
        return None


def get_provider_registry() -> ProviderRegistry:
    """Get or create the global provider registry singleton.

    Returns:
        The singleton ProviderRegistry instance.
    """
    global _registry
    if _registry is None:
        _registry = ProviderRegistry()
    return _registry

56	def format_cost_summary(
57	    model: str,
58	    total_tokens: int,
59	    total_cost: float,
60	    turn_tokens: int = 0,
61	    turn_cost: float = 0.0,
62	) -> str:
63	    """Format a complete cost summary.
64	
65	    Args:
66	        model: Current model name.
67	        total_tokens: Total session tokens.
68	        total_cost: Total session cost.
69	        turn_tokens: Current turn tokens.
70	        turn_cost: Current turn cost.
71	
72	    Returns:
73	        Formatted multi-line summary.
74	    """
75	    lines = [
76	        "# Cost Summary",
77	        "",
78	        f"  Model:          {model}",
79	        f"  Session tokens: {total_tokens:,}",
80	        f"  Session cost:   {format_cost(total_cost)}",
81	    ]
82	
83	    if turn_tokens > 0:
84	        lines.extend([
85	            f"  Turn tokens:    {turn_tokens:,}",
86	            f"  Turn cost:      {format_cost(turn_cost)}",
87	        ])
88	
89	    pricing = MODEL_PRICING.get(model)
90	    if pricing:
91	        lines.extend([
92	            "",
93	            f"  Pricing ({model}):",
94	            f"    Input:  ${pricing[0]:.3f} / 1M tokens",
95	            f"    Output: ${pricing[1]:.3f} / 1M tokens",
96	        ])
97	
98	    return "\n".join(lines)

"""Cost tracking utilities."""

from __future__ import annotations

# Pricing per 1M tokens: {model: (input_price, output_price)}
MODEL_PRICING: dict[str, tuple[float, float]] = {
    "claude-sonnet-4-20250514": (3.0, 15.0),
    "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022": (3.0, 15.0),
    "claude-3-haiku-20240307": (0.25, 1.25),
    "claude-3-opus-20240229": (15.0, 75.0),
    "gpt-4o": (2.50, 10.0),
    "gpt-4o-mini": (0.15, 0.60),
    "gpt-4-turbo": (10.0, 30.0),
    "gpt-3.5-turbo": (0.50, 1.50),
    "gemini-2.0-flash": (0.075, 0.30),
    "gemini-1.5-pro": (1.25, 5.0),
    "gemini-1.5-flash": (0.075, 0.30),
}


def calculate_cost(
    model: str,
    input_tokens: int,
    output_tokens: int,
) -> float:
    """Calculate the cost for a model invocation.

    Args:
        model: Model identifier.
        input_tokens: Number of input tokens.
        output_tokens: Number of output tokens.

    Returns:
        Cost in USD.
    """
    pricing = MODEL_PRICING.get(model, (0.0, 0.0))
    input_cost = (input_tokens / 1_000_000) * pricing[0]
    output_cost = (output_tokens / 1_000_000) * pricing[1]
    return input_cost + output_cost


def format_cost(cost: float) -> str:
    """Format a cost value for display.

    Args:
        cost: Cost in USD.

    Returns:
        Formatted string like '$0.0042'.
    """
    if cost < 0.01:
        return f"${cost:.4f}"
    return f"${cost:.2f}"


def format_cost_summary(
    model: str,
    total_tokens: int,
    total_cost: float,
    turn_tokens: int = 0,
    turn_cost: float = 0.0,
) -> str:
    """Format a complete cost summary.

    Args:
        model: Current model name.
        total_tokens: Total session tokens.
        total_cost: Total session cost.
        turn_tokens: Current turn tokens.
        turn_cost: Current turn cost.

    Returns:
        Formatted multi-line summary.
    """
    lines = [
        "# Cost Summary",
        "",
        f"  Model:          {model}",
        f"  Session tokens: {total_tokens:,}",
        f"  Session cost:   {format_cost(total_cost)}",
    ]

    if turn_tokens > 0:
        lines.extend([
            f"  Turn tokens:    {turn_tokens:,}",
            f"  Turn cost:      {format_cost(turn_cost)}",
        ])

    pricing = MODEL_PRICING.get(model)
    if pricing:
        lines.extend([
            "",
            f"  Pricing ({model}):",
            f"    Input:  ${pricing[0]:.3f} / 1M tokens",
            f"    Output: ${pricing[1]:.3f} / 1M tokens",
        ])

    return "\n".join(lines)

"""Git utility functions."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import os


async def run_git_command(args: str, working_directory: str = ".") -> str:
    """Run a git command and return its output.

    Args:
        args: Git subcommand and arguments (e.g. 'status --short').
        working_directory: Directory to run the command in.

    Returns:
        Combined stdout/stderr output.
    """
    cwd = os.path.expanduser(working_directory)

    try:
        process = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
            f"git {args}",
            stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
            stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
            cwd=cwd,
        )
        stdout, stderr = await asyncio.wait_for(process.communicate(), timeout=30)

        output = ""
        if stdout:
            output += stdout.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
        if stderr:
            output += stderr.decode("utf-8", errors="replace")

        return output.strip()

    except asyncio.TimeoutError:
        return "Error: Git command timed out."
    except Exception as e:
        return f"Error running git: {e}"


async def get_git_root(working_directory: str = ".") -> str | None:
    """Find the git repository root.

    Args:
        working_directory: Starting directory.

    Returns:
        Repository root path, or None if not in a git repo.
    """
    result = await run_git_command("rev-parse --show-toplevel", working_directory)
    if result.startswith("Error") or result.startswith("fatal"):
        return None
    return result.strip()


async def get_current_branch(working_directory: str = ".") -> str:
    """Get the current git branch name.

    Args:
        working_directory: Repository directory.

    Returns:
        Branch name or 'detached HEAD'.
    """
    result = await run_git_command("branch --show-current", working_directory)
    return result.strip() if result and not result.startswith("Error") else "detached HEAD"


async def get_git_status(working_directory: str = ".") -> str:
    """Get a short git status.

    Args:
        working_directory: Repository directory.

    Returns:
        Short status output.
    """
    return await run_git_command("status --short", working_directory)


async def is_git_repo(working_directory: str = ".") -> bool:
    """Check if the working directory is inside a git repository.

    Args:
        working_directory: Directory to check.

    Returns:
        True if inside a git repo.
    """
    root = await get_git_root(working_directory)
    return root is not None

"""Tool registry for managing available agent tools."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Optional

from langchain_core.tools import BaseTool

from tui_agent.tools.base import AgentTool

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_registry: Optional[ToolRegistry] = None


class ToolRegistry:
    """Registry managing all available tools for the agent.

    Lazily initializes built-in tools and supports registration
    of custom tools at runtime.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._tools: dict[str, AgentTool] = {}
        self._initialized = False

    def _ensure_initialized(self) -> None:
        """Lazily initialize all built-in tools."""
        if self._initialized:
            return
        self._initialized = True

        from tui_agent.tools.bash import BashTool
        from tui_agent.tools.file_edit import FileEditTool
        from tui_agent.tools.file_read import FileReadTool
        from tui_agent.tools.file_write import FileWriteTool
        from tui_agent.tools.glob_tool import GlobTool
        from tui_agent.tools.grep import GrepTool
        from tui_agent.tools.web_fetch import WebFetchTool
        from tui_agent.tools.web_search import WebSearchTool

        built_in: list[AgentTool] = [
            BashTool(),
            FileReadTool(),
            FileWriteTool(),
            FileEditTool(),
            GlobTool(),
            GrepTool(),
            WebFetchTool(),
            WebSearchTool(),
        ]

        for tool in built_in:
            self._tools[tool.name] = tool
            logger.debug("Registered tool: %s", tool.name)

    def register(self, tool: AgentTool) -> None:
        """Register a custom tool.

        Args:
            tool: Tool instance to register.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        self._tools[tool.name] = tool
        logger.info("Registered custom tool: %s", tool.name)

    def unregister(self, name: str) -> bool:
        """Unregister a tool by name.

        Args:
            name: Tool name to remove.

        Returns:
            True if the tool was found and removed.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        if name in self._tools:
            del self._tools[name]
            return True
        return False

    def get_tool(self, name: str) -> AgentTool | None:
        """Get a tool by name.

        Args:
            name: Tool identifier.

        Returns:
            Tool instance or None.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return self._tools.get(name)

    def list_tools(self) -> list[AgentTool]:
        """List all registered tools.

        Returns:
            List of all tool instances.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return list(self._tools.values())

    def get_langchain_tools(self) -> list[BaseTool]:
        """Get tools as LangChain BaseTool list for LLM binding.

        Returns:
            List of tools compatible with LangChain's bind_tools.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        return list(self._tools.values())

    def get_tools_by_category(self) -> dict[str, list[AgentTool]]:
        """Group tools by category.

        Returns:
            Dict mapping category name to list of tools.
        """
        self._ensure_initialized()
        categories: dict[str, list[AgentTool]] = {}
        for tool in self._tools.values():
            cat = getattr(tool, "category", "general")
            categories.setdefault(cat, []).append(tool)
        return categories


def get_tool_registry() -> ToolRegistry:
    """Get or create the global tool registry singleton.

    Returns:
        The singleton ToolRegistry instance.
    """
    global _registry
    if _registry is None:
        _registry = ToolRegistry()
    return _registry

"""Conversation state management with LangGraph checkpointing."""

from __future__ import annotations

import uuid
from typing import Any

from langchain_core.messages import BaseMessage, HumanMessage, AIMessage, SystemMessage


class ConversationManager:
    """Manages conversation state and thread IDs for LangGraph checkpointing.

    Each conversation gets a unique thread_id. The checkpointer handles
    actual state persistence; this class manages the thread lifecycle.
    """

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self._thread_id: str = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self._turn_count: int = 0

    @property
    def thread_id(self) -> str:
        """Current conversation thread ID."""
        return self._thread_id

    @property
    def turn_count(self) -> int:
        """Number of user turns in this conversation."""
        return self._turn_count

    def new_thread(self) -> str:
        """Start a new conversation thread.

        Returns:
            The new thread ID.
        """
        self._thread_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        self._turn_count = 0
        return self._thread_id

    def resume_thread(self, thread_id: str) -> None:
        """Resume an existing conversation thread.

        Args:
            thread_id: Thread ID to resume.
        """
        self._thread_id = thread_id

    def increment_turn(self) -> int:
        """Increment and return the turn count.

        Returns:
            Updated turn count.
        """
        self._turn_count += 1
        return self._turn_count

    def get_config(self) -> dict[str, Any]:
        """Get LangGraph config for the current thread.

        Returns:
            Config dict with thread_id.
        """
        return {"configurable": {"thread_id": self._thread_id}}

    @staticmethod
    def summarize_messages(messages: list[BaseMessage], keep_recent: int = 10) -> list[BaseMessage]:
        """Create a condensed version of the message history.

        Keeps the system message and the most recent N messages,
        replacing older messages with a summary placeholder.

        Args:
            messages: Full message history.
            keep_recent: Number of recent messages to keep verbatim.

        Returns:
            Condensed message list.
        """
        if len(messages) <= keep_recent + 1:
            return messages

        # Keep system message if present
        system_msgs = [m for m in messages if isinstance(m, SystemMessage)]
        non_system = [m for m in messages if not isinstance(m, SystemMessage)]

        if len(non_system) <= keep_recent:
            return messages

        old_messages = non_system[:-keep_recent]
        recent_messages = non_system[-keep_recent:]

        # Create summary of old messages
        summary_parts: list[str] = []
        for msg in old_messages:
            if isinstance(msg, HumanMessage):
                content = str(msg.content)[:100]
                summary_parts.append(f"User: {content}")
            elif isinstance(msg, AIMessage):
                content = str(msg.content)[:100]
                summary_parts.append(f"Assistant: {content}")

        summary_text = (
            "[Earlier conversation summary]\n" + "\n".join(summary_parts[-20:])
        )
        summary_msg = SystemMessage(content=summary_text)

        return system_msgs + [summary_msg] + recent_messages

1	"""Token counting utilities."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import logging
6	from typing import Any
7	
8	from langchain_core.messages import BaseMessage
9	
10	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
11	
12	# Approximate tokens per character for fallback estimation
13	CHARS_PER_TOKEN = 4
14	
15	
16	def count_message_tokens(messages: list[BaseMessage], model: str = "") -> int:
17	    """Count tokens in a list of messages.
18	
19	    Uses tiktoken for OpenAI models, falls back to character-based
20	    estimation for other providers.
21	
22	    Args:
23	        messages: Messages to count tokens for.
24	        model: Model name for tokenizer selection.
25	
26	    Returns:
27	        Estimated token count.
28	    """
29	    total_text = ""
30	    for msg in messages:
31	        content = msg.content
32	        if isinstance(content, str):
33	            total_text += content
34	        elif isinstance(content, list):
35	            for block in content:
36	                if isinstance(block, dict) and "text" in block:
37	                    total_text += block["text"]
38	                elif isinstance(block, str):
39	                    total_text += block
40	
41	    # Try tiktoken for OpenAI models
42	    if "gpt" in model or "o1" in model:
43	        try:
44	            import tiktoken
45	            try:
46	                enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
47	            except KeyError:
48	                enc = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
49	            return len(enc.encode(total_text))
50	        except ImportError:
51	            pass
52	
53	    # Fallback: character-based estimation
54	    return len(total_text) // CHARS_PER_TOKEN
55	
56	
57	def count_string_tokens(text: str, model: str = "") -> int:
58	    """Count tokens in a plain string.
59	
60	    Args:
61	        text: Text to count tokens for.
62	        model: Model name for tokenizer selection.
63	
64	    Returns:
65	        Estimated token count.
66	    """
67	    if "gpt" in model or "o1" in model:
68	        try:
69	            import tiktoken
70	            try:
71	                enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
72	            except KeyError:
73	                enc = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
74	            return len(enc.encode(text))
75	        except ImportError:
76	            pass
77	
78	    return len(text) // CHARS_PER_TOKEN

"""Token counting utilities."""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
from typing import Any

from langchain_core.messages import BaseMessage

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

# Approximate tokens per character for fallback estimation
CHARS_PER_TOKEN = 4


def count_message_tokens(messages: list[BaseMessage], model: str = "") -> int:
    """Count tokens in a list of messages.

    Uses tiktoken for OpenAI models, falls back to character-based
    estimation for other providers.

    Args:
        messages: Messages to count tokens for.
        model: Model name for tokenizer selection.

    Returns:
        Estimated token count.
    """
    total_text = ""
    for msg in messages:
        content = msg.content
        if isinstance(content, str):
            total_text += content
        elif isinstance(content, list):
            for block in content:
                if isinstance(block, dict) and "text" in block:
                    total_text += block["text"]
                elif isinstance(block, str):
                    total_text += block

    # Try tiktoken for OpenAI models
    if "gpt" in model or "o1" in model:
        try:
            import tiktoken
            try:
                enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
            except KeyError:
                enc = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
            return len(enc.encode(total_text))
        except ImportError:
            pass

    # Fallback: character-based estimation
    return len(total_text) // CHARS_PER_TOKEN


def count_string_tokens(text: str, model: str = "") -> int:
    """Count tokens in a plain string.

    Args:
        text: Text to count tokens for.
        model: Model name for tokenizer selection.

    Returns:
        Estimated token count.
    """
    if "gpt" in model or "o1" in model:
        try:
            import tiktoken
            try:
                enc = tiktoken.encoding_for_model(model)
            except KeyError:
                enc = tiktoken.get_encoding("cl100k_base")
            return len(enc.encode(text))
        except ImportError:
            pass

    return len(text) // CHARS_PER_TOKEN


def estimate_remaining_context(
    current_tokens: int,
    model: str = "",
    context_window: int | None = None,
) -> int:
    """Estimate remaining context window capacity.

    Args:
        current_tokens: Current token usage.
        model: Model name for context window lookup.
        context_window: Override context window size.

    Returns:
        Estimated remaining tokens.
    """
    if context_window is None:
        windows: dict[str, int] = {
            "claude-sonnet-4-20250514": 200_000,
            "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022": 200_000,
            "claude-3-haiku-20240307": 200_000,
            "claude-3-opus-20240229": 200_000,
            "gpt-4o": 128_000,
            "gpt-4o-mini": 128_000,
            "gpt-4-turbo": 128_000,
            "gemini-2.0-flash": 1_000_000,
            "gemini-1.5-pro": 2_000_000,
        }
        context_window = windows.get(model, 128_000)

    return max(0, context_window - current_tokens)

"""Main REPL application - Claude Code style terminal interface using LangGraph."""

from __future__ import annotations

import asyncio
import os
import subprocess
import sys
import time
from typing import Any

from rich.console import Console
from rich.live import Live
from rich.markdown import Markdown
from rich.panel import Panel
from rich.spinner import Spinner
from rich.text import Text

from tui_agent.config import Config
from tui_agent.utils.cost import calculate_cost, format_cost


class TUIAgentApp:
    """Claude Code style REPL terminal agent powered by LangGraph."""

    def __init__(self, config: Config, resume_thread: str | None = None, **kwargs: Any) -> None:
        self.config = config
        self.console = Console()
        self._is_processing = False
        self._session_start = time.time()
        self._tool_count = 0
        self._total_tokens = 0
        self._total_cost = 0.0
        self._history: list[str] = []
        self._graph: Any = None
        self._thread_id: str = resume_thread or "default"
        self._current_model = config.default_model
        self._current_provider = config.default_provider

    def run(self) -> None:
        """Main REPL loop."""
        self.console.clear()
        self._print_welcome()

        # Initialize agent graph
        self.console.print("[dim]Initializing LangGraph agent...[/dim]")
        try:
            asyncio.get_event_loop().run_until_complete(self._initialize())
            self.console.print(
                f"[green]Ready![/green] Model: [bold cyan]{self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}[/bold cyan]\n"
            )
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"[red]Failed to initialize: {e}[/red]")
            self.console.print("[dim]Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, or GOOGLE_API_KEY[/dim]")
            return

        # REPL loop
        while True:
            try:
                self._print_status_bar()
                user_input = self._get_input()
                if user_input is None:
                    break
                if not user_input.strip():
                    continue

                self._history.append(user_input)

                # Handle slash commands
                if user_input.startswith("/"):
                    self._handle_command(user_input)
                    continue

                # Run agent
                self._run_agent(user_input)

            except KeyboardInterrupt:
                if self._is_processing:
                    self.console.print("\n[yellow]Interrupted.[/yellow]")
                    self._is_processing = False
                else:
                    self.console.print("\n[dim]Press Ctrl+C again or type /exit to quit[/dim]")
                    try:
                        time.sleep(0.5)
                    except KeyboardInterrupt:
                        self.console.print("\n[dim]Goodbye![/dim]")
                        break
            except EOFError:
                self.console.print("\n[dim]Goodbye![/dim]")
                break

    async def _initialize(self) -> None:
        """Initialize the LangGraph agent graph."""
        from tui_agent.graph.agent import create_agent_graph
        from tui_agent.graph.checkpointer import create_checkpointer

        checkpointer = await create_checkpointer(self.config.db_path)
        self._graph = create_agent_graph(self.config, checkpointer)

    def _print_welcome(self) -> None:
        """Print welcome banner."""
        self.console.print(
            Panel(
                "[bold]TUI Code Agent[/bold] - LangGraph-powered coding assistant\n"
                "[dim]Type a message, /help for commands, Ctrl+C twice to exit[/dim]",
                border_style="blue",
            )
        )

    def _print_status_bar(self) -> None:
        """Print Claude Code style status bar."""
        branch = self._get_branch()
        elapsed = int(time.time() - self._session_start)
        mins, secs = divmod(elapsed, 60)
        time_str = f"{mins}m{secs:02d}s" if mins else f"{secs}s"

        if self._total_tokens > 1000:
            token_str = f"{self._total_tokens/1000:.1f}k"
        else:
            token_str = str(self._total_tokens)

        status = Text()
        status.append(f"  branch:", style="dim")
        status.append(f"{branch}", style="bold")
        status.append(f"\n  {self._current_provider}:", style="dim")
        status.append(f"{self._current_model}", style="bold cyan")
        status.append(f" | session:{time_str}", style="dim")
        status.append(f" | tokens:{token_str}", style="dim")
        status.append(f" | {format_cost(self._total_cost)}", style="dim")
        status.append(f" | tools:{self._tool_count}", style="dim")

        self.console.print(status)

    def _get_branch(self) -> str:
        """Get current git branch (sync)."""
        try:
            result = subprocess.run(
                ["git", "branch", "--show-current"],
                capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5
            )
            branch = result.stdout.strip()
            return branch if branch else "no-git"
        except Exception:
            return "no-git"

    def _get_input(self) -> str | None:
        """Get user input with prompt."""
        self.console.print("  [dim]" + "─" * 40 + "[/dim]")
        try:
            user_input = input("  ❯ ")
            self.console.print("  [dim]" + "─" * 40 + "[/dim]")
            return user_input
        except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
            raise

    def _run_agent(self, user_input: str) -> None:
        """Run the agent graph and display streaming response."""
        self._is_processing = True

        loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
        try:
            loop.run_until_complete(self._run_agent_async(user_input))
        except KeyboardInterrupt:
            self.console.print("\n[yellow]Interrupted.[/yellow]")
        except Exception as e:
            self.console.print(f"\n  [red]Agent error: {e}[/red]")
        finally:
            loop.close()
            self._is_processing = False

    async def _run_agent_async(self, user_input: str) -> None:
        """Run the LangGraph agent with streaming output."""
        from langchain_core.messages import AIMessage, HumanMessage, ToolMessage

        if self._graph is None:
            self.console.print("[red]Agent not initialized.[/red]")
            return

        graph_config = {"configurable": {"thread_id": self._thread_id}}
        inputs = {
            "messages": [HumanMessage(content=user_input)],
            "current_model": self._current_model,
            "current_provider": self._current_provider,
            "working_directory": self.config.working_directory,
        }

        response_text = ""
        first_token = True

        with Live(
            Spinner("dots", text="[bold cyan]Brewing...[/bold cyan]"),
            console=self.console,
            refresh_per_second=10,
            transient=True,
        ) as live:
            spinner_active = True
            try:
                async for event in self._graph.astream_events(
                    inputs, config=graph_config, version="v2"
                ):
                    kind = event.get("event", "")

                    if kind == "on_chat_model_stream":
                        chunk = event.get("data", {}).get("chunk")
                        if chunk and hasattr(chunk, "content"):
                            content = chunk.content
                            # Handle both str and list content blocks
                            text = ""
                            if isinstance(content, str):
                                text = content
                            elif isinstance(content, list):
                                for block in content:
                                    if isinstance(block, dict):
                                        text += block.get("text", "")
                                    elif isinstance(block, str):
                                        text += block
                                    elif hasattr(block, "text"):
                                        text += str(block.text)

                            if text:
                                if first_token:
                                    first_token = False
                                    live.stop()
                                    spinner_active = False
                                response_text += text
                                print(text, end="", flush=True)

                    elif kind == "on_tool_start":
                        tool_name = event.get("name", "unknown")
                        self._tool_count += 1
                        if spinner_active:
                            live.update(
                                Spinner("dots", text=f"[bold cyan]Running {tool_name}...[/bold cyan]")
                            )
                        else:
                            self.console.print(
                                f"\n  [magenta]⚙ {tool_name}[/magenta]", end=""
                            )

                    elif kind == "on_tool_end":
                        output = event.get("data", {}).get("output", "")
                        if not spinner_active:
                            out_str = str(output)[:100].replace("\n", " ")
                            self.console.print(f" [dim]→ {out_str}[/dim]")

                    elif kind == "on_chain_end":
                        # Extract token/cost data from chain output if available
                        output = event.get("data", {}).get("output", {})
                        if isinstance(output, dict):
                            if "total_tokens" in output:
                                self._total_tokens = output["total_tokens"]
                            if "total_cost" in output:
                                self._total_cost = output["total_cost"]

            except Exception as e:
                if spinner_active:
                    live.stop()
                self.console.print(f"\n  [red]Error: {e}[/red]")

        # End the response with newline
        if response_text:
            print()  # newline after streaming
            self.console.print()

    def _handle_command(self, cmd: str) -> None:
        """Handle slash commands."""
        parts = cmd.strip().split(maxsplit=1)
        command = parts[0].lower()
        args = parts[1] if len(parts) > 1 else ""

        if command in ("/help", "/h"):
            self.console.print(Panel(
                "[bold]Commands:[/bold]\n"
                "  /help     - Show this help\n"
                "  /model    - Show or switch model (e.g. /model openai:gpt-4o)\n"
                "  /cost     - Show token usage and cost\n"
                "  /clear    - Clear screen\n"
                "  /compact  - Reset conversation\n"
                "  /exit     - Exit",
                title="Help",
                border_style="blue",
            ))
        elif command == "/model":
            if args:
                if ":" in args:
                    provider, model = args.split(":", 1)
                else:
                    provider = self._current_provider
                    model = args
                self._current_provider = provider
                self._current_model = model
                # Reinitialize graph with new model
                try:
                    self.config.default_provider = provider
                    self.config.default_model = model
                    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
                    loop.run_until_complete(self._initialize())
                    loop.close()
                    self.console.print(f"[green]Switched to {provider}:{model}[/green]")
                except Exception as e:
                    self.console.print(f"[red]Failed: {e}[/red]")
            else:
                self.console.print(
                    f"Current: [bold cyan]{self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}[/bold cyan]\n"
                    f"[dim]Usage: /model provider:model (e.g. /model openai:gpt-4o)[/dim]"
                )
        elif command == "/cost":
            self.console.print(Panel(
                f"Model:         {self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}\n"
                f"Total tokens:  {self._total_tokens:,}\n"
                f"Total cost:    {format_cost(self._total_cost)}",
                title="Usage",
                border_style="green",
            ))
        elif command == "/clear":
            self.console.clear()
        elif command == "/compact":
            import uuid
            self._thread_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            self.console.print("[green]Conversation reset (new thread).[/green]")
        elif command in ("/exit", "/quit", "/q"):
            self.console.print("[dim]Goodbye![/dim]")
            sys.exit(0)
        else:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Unknown command: {command}. Try /help[/yellow]")

                "  /exit     - Exit",
                title="Help",
                border_style="blue",
            ))
        elif command == "/model":
            if args:
                if ":" in args:
                    provider, model = args.split(":", 1)
                else:
                    provider = self._current_provider
                    model = args
                self._current_provider = provider
                self._current_model = model
                # Reinitialize graph with new model
                try:
                    self.config.default_provider = provider
                    self.config.default_model = model
                    loop = asyncio.new_event_loop()
                    loop.run_until_complete(self._initialize())
                    loop.close()
                    self.console.print(f"[green]Switched to {provider}:{model}[/green]")
                except Exception as e:
                    self.console.print(f"[red]Failed: {e}[/red]")
            else:
                self.console.print(
                    f"Current: [bold cyan]{self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}[/bold cyan]\n"
                    f"[dim]Usage: /model provider:model (e.g. /model openai:gpt-4o)[/dim]"
                )
        elif command == "/cost":
            self.console.print(Panel(
                f"Model:         {self._current_provider}:{self._current_model}\n"
                f"Total tokens:  {self._total_tokens:,}\n"
                f"Total cost:    {format_cost(self._total_cost)}",
                title="Usage",
                border_style="green",
            ))
        elif command == "/clear":
            self.console.clear()
        elif command == "/compact":
            import uuid
            self._thread_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            self.console.print("[green]Conversation reset (new thread).[/green]")
        elif command in ("/exit", "/quit", "/q"):
            self.console.print("[dim]Goodbye![/dim]")
            sys.exit(0)
        else:
            self.console.print(f"[yellow]Unknown command: {command}. Try /help[/yellow]")
